======================================================================
PAGE: Ahuatotli, the Blind
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Ahuatotli,_the_Blind
======================================================================

Ahuatotli, the Blind
Ahuatotli, the Blind
Location(s)
Vaal Nexus
(
The Grand Architect's Temple
)
Resistance(s)
30%
Fire
30%
Cold
30%
Lightning
15%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
80% Less Curse Effectiveness
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
Immune To Knockback
Unwavering
Ahuatotli, the Blind
is a boss that can only appear inside
The Grand Architect's Temple
nodes within the
Vaal Outpost
biome, inside
The Azurite Mine
in
Delve
.
Arena
The arena for the fight is called
Vaal Nexus
. The arena is X-shaped, with the outer areas darkened. The outer areas contain four Beam Pyramids which create beams that spin, dealing physical damage and applying
Corrupted Blood
. Each of the pyramids can be disabled by stepping on a pressure plate behind it.
Skills
Ahuatotli, the Blind has several different
skills
, focused solely on physical damage.
Basic Attack - Winds up and shoots a blast that strikes a small area, dealing physical damage.
Blood Explosion - Winds up, and creates a circle that deals physical damage over time when stepped on. Lasts 5 seconds.
Spirit bomb - Creates a large blob that explodes shortly after, dealing physical damage.
Fire Blood Meteors - Spawns 20 blobs around the arena, which strike the ground shortly after.
Fire Blood Meteors - Ahuatotli levitates and fires a barrage of physical damage projectiles aimed at the ground.
Expanding Ring - Only used after Ahuatotli is below 50% health. After a wind-up signal, creates an expanding nova that deals heavy physical damage and spans the entire lit section of the arena.
At 50% health, Ahuatotli darkens the arena and becomes invulnerable. Vaal monsters spawn from 4 directions and small circles of light appear and disappear during the phase.
Items found from this monster

Version history
Version
Changes
3.15.0
The tier four Aisling crafting bench reward that adds a Veiled modifier now first removes a random modifier.
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
(Not documented in patch note) The reward that add one or two veiled modifiers from Aisling , has been moved to tier 4. The previous tiers now rewards
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
3.10.0
Aisling can now award Metamorph Scarabs if she has the Intervention Job. She will now award Double-Veiled Weapons and Jewellery if she's in Transportation, and Double-Veiled Armour and Jewellery if she's in Fortification.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
(not in patch notes) Minimum area level to encounter Aisling has changed to Act 9 onwards.
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
Several veiled modifiers now have fewer tiers. Low tiers contribute more towards unlocking higher tiers, however it will, on average, take more unveils to unlock the highest tier.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.

PAGE: Al-Hezmin, the Hunter

Al-Hezmin, the Hunter
Al-Hezmin, the Hunter
Level(s)
68 / 83
Location(s)
Hunter's Ambush
Quest(s)
The Hunter

1 668 108 / 32 578 382
Damage
2092.9 / 5038.9

2
Resistance(s)
50%
Fire
50%
Cold
50%
Lightning
30%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
The triangular arena of Al-Hezmin
Al-Hezmin, the Hunter
is one of the Elderslayers /
Conquerors
of the Atlas. He serves as one of the end-game bosses of the Atlas. The boss fight itself has 8 different levels, based on the player's current Atlas Awakening level. It is colloquially known as
A1 Hezmin
through
A8 Hezmin
.
Items influenced by the Hunter have a green icon on its header
and have unique
Hunter modifiers
.
Non-influenced items can have the Hunter's influence added using a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, which is a rare drop from Al-Hezmin.
Defeating Al-Hezmin for the first item in a region will drop a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
that can be socketed into
Atlas citadels
. There are 8 atlas regions thus there are 8 Viridian Watchstones.
Encounter
Players must first spawn a Conqueror within the Atlas using the steps in the
Atlas quick guide
. Then talking to Kirac and Zana will open a portal to the Conqueror's citadel. Defeating the map boss will open a portal to the Conqueror's arena, where they must fight Al-Hezmin.
Al-Hezmin's arena is a triangular area with a triangular acid pool in the center. Depending on which phase he's on, it's better or worse to stay inside the triangle.
Tips
Al-Hezmin is notable for dealing a high amount of
Chaos
damage, and is also the only Conqueror to deal this type of damage.
Players must keep moving to avoid the projectile attacks of the Vipers and Wyverns spawned by Al-Hezmin, and attack him as and when he becomes vulnerable.
Constant mobility during the fight is recommended, but avoid entering the acid pool as it can be deadly in some phases.
Attacks
Overview of attacks:
Default melee attack
Sweeps the area in front of him with both his swords and launches a long range projectile in front of him.
Performs a stab combo, lunging forward then finishes with a slam that deals damage around him.
Fires a volley of poisonous projectiles. He is more likely to use this attack if you are far away from him.
Launches a wide wyvern-like projectile at the player. Afterwards, the center of the pool will be filled with poison. Then, three invincible vipers will emerge from the poison pool, firing projectiles that deal area damage and apply a very strong
poison
on hit. Al-Hezmin will attempt to constantly run away from the player during this phase.
Causes Tunneling Vipers to appear from
outside
of the triangle. They will only appear if you get close enough to them. They will also launch poison projectiles, but deal less damage.

Lore
Main page:
Al-Hezmin's journals
Al-Hezmin's journals are clickable lore items dropped each time you defeat Al-Hezmin.
From
Zana
:
When you spend as much time in the Atlas as we did, basic survival skills no longer suffice, and it becomes necessary to master the lay of the land. That's a tall ask in a place where the land changes from day to day, and yet Al-Hezmin took to it like a rhoa to mud. I could not count the number of times he saved us from getting lost in endless twisting caverns, or spotted the tracks of a dangerous beast far sooner than the rest of us would have.
Our praise must have gone to his head, because he began to strive to be the best at all times. If Drox brought back two boars from a hunt, he needed to bring back three. In battle, he had to deal the flashiest and most devastating blows, and he had to make sure we all saw him do it.
His fixation on honing his skills was actually quite useful during our campaign against the Elder, so at the time we thought nothing of it, but... It hollowed him out in some fundamental way. He was full of bravado, yet at the same time desperately afraid of being exposed as merely second or third best. He was never unkind to me, even as the madness crept upon him, but for capable warriors like Drox that threatened his self-image... A clash was inevitable. We slipped away in the dead of night, but I doubt we've escaped a man of his talents. He's out there, lurking and watching, waiting for the best time to strike...
See also
Sirus, Awakener of Worlds
Baran, the Crusader
Drox, the Warlord
Veritania, the Redeemer
Basilisk Supporter Pack

Albino Rhoa

The Mud Flats

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
The Albino Rhoa cannot be raised as a
spectre
using the

skill although it can be raised as a
zombie
using the
Raise Zombie
skill.

unique map, despite

Amanamu, Liege of the Lightless
Amanamu, Liege of the Lightless
Amanamu, Liege of the Lightless
Location(s)
Abyssal Depths
Resistance(s)
30%
Fire
30%
Cold
30%
Lightning
15%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
80% Less Curse Effectiveness
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
Immune To Knockback
Unwavering
Amanamu, Liege of the Lightless
is a boss that can appear in the
Abyssal Depths
.
Arena
Skills
Summon Stygian Hearts: Summons Stygian Hearts, which makes Amanamu invulnerable. These must be destroyed to be able to damage Amanamu.
Shard Blast: Channels and fires waves of shards at the player. Can be dodged by circling around.
Add Spawn: Turns dark and invulnerable and summons monsters.
Shard Spray: Fires many shards at the player, which stick to the ground. These shards will detonate to deal damage after a delay.
At 75% health, the boss will retreat into a sub-zone containing a
Stygian Spire
. It must be destroyed within 90 seconds or the boss will escape. This process repeats at 50% and 25% health, with the timer carrying over.

Version
Changes
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.7.0
Improved the early-game experience. Fights in the first three Acts will be tougher but more responsive, involved and dynamic. This includes fights against basic monsters!
2.4.2
Amarissa and Ambrosia in the Caverns of Wrath and Anger now have new visual effects.
0.9.10
Reduced damage of
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
when used by
Goat Shamans
and Amarissa, Daughter of Merveil.
0.9.9
Merveil
has two daughters,
Ambrosia
and Amarissa, who inhabit her caves. They are fire and lightning themed, respectively.

PAGE: Arakaali, Spinner of Shadows
CATEGORIES: Act 7 unique monsters, Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Arakaali,_Spinner_of_Shadows
======================================================================

Arakaali, Spinner of Shadows
Arakaali, Spinner of Shadows
Level(s)
55
Location(s)
The Temple of Decay Level 2
Quest(s)
The Mother of Spiders
Resistance(s)
Resists
Elemental Damage
Resists
Chaos Damage
Skill(s)
Laser
Laser Barrage
Mortar
Cleave
Hide
Arakaali, Spinner of Shadows
is the final boss of
act 7
, located in
the Temple of Decay Level 2
.
It is an ancient
Vaal
goddess according to the quest log and NPC dialogues. It is also worshipped by people of
Azmerian
descent.
Quest
Defeating her is part of the quest
The Mother of Spiders
. The rescue
Silk
part of the quest, however, is scripted to be fail.
Strategy
Abilities
The boss can applies
Covered in Webs
debuff.
Lore
There are
Shrines to Arakaali
scattered around
Act 7
. The shrines have lore regarding Arakaali.
There are other NPC dialogues regarding Arakaali:
From Sin:
"
A temptress and a predator.
Vaal
legends say she crawled up from the blackest of pits during the creation of the world.
No, her beginnings were far more mundane, a mortal harlot whose endless lust for loin and lecherous delight saw her transformed into the very image of her dark desires.
'The Spinner of Shadows' they once called her. She sees herself as a regular goddess of love, and has the romantically forged temple to prove it.
That's where you'll find her. Yet there's little romance to the lady herself. At least, I doubt the corpses that now embrace her carapace would think so.
"
- Sin - "Arakaali" dialogue
"
Answering the call of a royal invitation, I visited the Spinner of Shadows as an emissary for a small and fragile alliance of gods. Mostly weak deities huddling together in terror of being consumed by their greaters. At this time, Queen Arakaali ruled an empire, and so invited me to gaze upon her mighty works with appropriate wonder.
If I'd looked past this pretense, I may have chanced to see her hidden desire to have me share her bed. For years I lay trapped in her webbed sheets. Some days she enjoyed my prowess, other days we enjoyed each other.
Yet, this illusion of love and leisure simply veiled the morbid reality that I was not free to leave. I languished under her bewitching spell until the day the spider was betrayed by her own flies, and sealed within that temple of her own fevered making.
"
- Sin - "The Spinner of Shadows" dialogue
From Eramir:
"
From what I can recall, Arakaali was a Vaal fertility goddess, a rather unsettling union of sexuality and mortality. Whilst usually presenting herself as a large arachnid, Arakaali would often assume human form, a ruse intended to lure mortals into the act of copulation. The entries were vague about the gender of her prey.
After satiating her carnal desires, she would then quench her divine thirst, draining her erstwhile lover of all bodily fluid. Her acolytes would then collect the dessicated husk and give it a decorative placement in Arakaali's unholy temple.
I fear that
Silk
knows not the true nature of the 'marriage' he so desperately seeks.
"
- Eramir "Arakaali" dialogue
From Yeena:
"
I have asked questions of Spirit and it has answered in dreams that wake me with screaming. Arakaali will suck all life from this land, leave only empty husks and dusty bones. There will be no Spirit, no us, no thing left to love and laugh.
Only husks and dust...and Arakaali.
"
- Yeena "Arakaali" dialogue
After killing Arakaali:
"
Many an artist of
Azmerian
descent has engaged in the sad worship of that sultry arachnid, but you looked her in those hypnotic eyes, and then ripped them out. She lies in her bed of sordid memory... alone.
A little of this spider yet creeps and crawls in my palm. A touch more power for my subduer of gods?
"
- Sin "Arakaali" dialogue after its death
"
You hear that? No? It is Spirit singing as does the fly who escapes the web. Yes, it is most happy with us... and with
your strange friend
there too.
He is a god, yes I can see that much. But why he helps you? Of all questions, that one you must have answer to.
Now I must return to
Broken Bridge
... so tired now. We will speak more of Silk and sorrow then.
"
- Yeena, after the death of Arakaali
Video
Version history
Version
Changes
3.0.0
Added to the game

Archbishop Geofri the Abashed
is a
unique
skeleton
located in
The Crypt Level 2
in
Act 2
. He guards the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
for the
Through Sacred Ground
quest. Killing him is not required, though.
Lore
Templar and theologian, Geofri was a stoic of the Eternal Faith. He deplored anything thaumaturgical, and was therefore disgusted when
Chitus
installed Maligaro’s laboratory into the Phrecian Forest, not a stone’s throw from Geofri’s Phrecia Cathedral (now
The Fellshrine Ruins
). It was Geofri who coined the title, “
Chamber of Sins
” for Maligaro’s lab.
Geofri plotted with High Templar
Voll
to destroy
Maligaro
and his work, but Maligaro got wind of the building threat. Maligaro’s pre-emptive strike put an end to the plot, and to Geofri who was slain with Maligaro’s Etcher at the foot of Saint Corutino the Golden Hand’s Shrine.
Archbishop Geofri remains in Fellshrine to this day, animated by The Twist into a Brittle Templar of some power.
Map variants
A stronger version of Archbishop Geofri the Abashed is featured in the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, known as
Litanius, the Black Prayer
.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.1.0
Added 32 new maps including two new Unique maps.
The entire Atlas has been reworked. Many map names have changed, as have their balance, tier and position on the Atlas. Many maps have also had their boss fights swapped or completely reworked.
The rules used to generate monsters in endgame maps have been significantly reworked and rebalanced around the rate at which we expect players to reliably clear out the map. In general, narrow, linear maps will contain fewer total monsters than maps with complex layouts. We will be closely monitoring and adjusting the balance of this change and expect to make further changes and improvements over time.

is the final boss of

(Level 3 Royal Meeting Room).

The Apex of Sacrifice
:

the Temple of Atzoatl
in the Throne of Atziri (Tier 3 Royal Meeting Room).

Uber Atziri
), players must use the following fragments in their map device, which form the
The Alluring Abyss
:

Note
:
"Attacker" does not imply that only damage from attacks gets reflected − spells are also included.
Video guide
Strategy
Atziri is one of the most challenging bosses in the game, with her base form rivaling lower red tier maps and her enhanced version rivaling endgame bosses. Strong well rounded defenses are an absolute must, as is
reflect
mitigation or immunity if your build doesn't deal indirect damage like
traps
/
mines
,
minions
,
totems
, etc.
Normal Phase
Initially, Atziri is alone. In this form, she has
built-in damage
reflect
, making her dangerous to fight against if your build deals too much direct damage. Moreover, she reflects
curses
, meaning the player gets cursed as well.
Atziri can use the following attacks:
An arc of spears that apply
bleed
. The damage from bleed is not too high, unless you have no physical mitigation. This attack is much faster in the
the Alluring Abyss
version.
A single
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
with short channeling. This attack can easily kill characters with health around 5k or lower instantly without mitigation, and even against characters with more effective life, if it
ignites
you the amount of damage will be massive. This spell has 20% increased radius in
the Alluring Abyss
version.
A Chain of randomly placed
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
s dealing lightning damage. Each storm call is marked with an orange spell circle and explodes after 3 seconds of being placed, in quick succession. The duration of storm calls placed exceeds 3 seconds, so this means that new storm calls are cast amidst the starting chain of explosions.
She can also use these attacks at lower health:
Multiple randomly placed empowered
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
with a massive
AoE
radius. This spell penetrates 8% percent Fire Resistance (10% in the
the Alluring Abyss
version). This spell can be withstood with a large effective HP Pool and Ruby Flask + any other forms of increasing maximum fire resist. This quantity of flameblasts increases for every additional player in the instance. This skill can easily kill most characters in a single hit and should be avoided with a movement skill at all costs.
A Chain of randomly placed empowered
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
s dealing lightning damage. Each storm call is marked with a thick yellow circle and a wider thin yellow line. The thin yellow exterior line is the true radius of the spell. This spell behaves the same as the orange storm call but deals 50% more damage. This spell should be absolutely avoided and Topaz Flask may be required as a safe measure.
Add Phase
Atziri shields herself, preventing her from taking damage. She summons minions that walk towards her and heal her when they reach her. The minions will not attack the player. If the minions manage to reach Atziri, she will gain some
energy shield
. This is by far the safest phase and a good time for recovering
flask
charges.
Atziri's
energy shield
will recharge while shielding. The best way keep the minions from healing her is to stand as close to Atziri as possible and use an
Area of Effect
skill to clear the minions as fast as possible. refilling
flasks
, regenerating
life
, recharging
energy shield
or to curse Atziri (don't forget to remove the reflect).
Atziri can still draw aggression of your minions, so it's better to avoid staying too close to her if you are a minion-based build..
Split Phase
Atziri splits into four copies of herself. This phase happens at fixed intervals: only when she loses a quarter of her life (so at 75%, 50% and 25%). If the minions heal her back to one of those life levels, she will not do this phase again. One of the four copies needs to be defeated in order to be able to damage Atziri again:
One holds a spear in her hand that fires a single projectile at the player; dealing
bleeding
damage over time.
One has fire on her hand and casts a single
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
on top of the player's current location (Similar to first form's single flameblast).
One has lightning on her hand and casts a chain of randomly placed
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
One has a mirror and
reflects
100% of physical and elemental damage taken (see
this modifier
). Attacking this copy with direct damage is consequently fatal and only an option for characters that deal indirect or pure chaos damage and/or for characters that cannot take reflected damage (
Slayer
's
Headsman
,
Elementalist
's
Paragon of Calamity
,
Assassin
's
Opportunistic
).
The spear has very poor aim and a moderate amount of damage. As long as the bleed is under control, the skill is largely ignored. Because the flameblast is always cast on top of the player (with the same cast time and delay), it is essential for players to get used to the rhythm of each cast. Melee players, in particular, can draw out her flameblast in a random location and attack one of Atziri copies during the cast time and delay.
Traps
,
mines
, and pure chaos damage skills like
Essence Drain
can attack the mirror safely while keeping their distance from the other clones.

Lore
Atziri was the Queen of Vaal and reigned over the Vaal Empire. Her thaumaturgist was
Doryani
.
"
Atziri was the last queen of the Vaal civilization. She was extremely vain and held court in a room lined with mirrors. Atziri wished for eternal youth, inspired by the serial killer Zerphi living 168 years with a young body, and her thaumaturgist Doryani sacrificed countless victims trying to achieve this. Destroyed by the Cataclysm, she still lingers in a nightmare realm that is leaking corruption back into the land of Wraeclast.
"
- Official Forums Chris (March 1, 2014). "
Path of Exile: Sacrifice of the Vaal Full Information
".
Official Path of Exile Forums
.
The base type
Sacrificial Garb
obviously refers to the skins of the sacrificed ones. The flavour text from
Shadowstitch
and
Transcendent Spirit
further indicate that.
"
Take comfort knowing that, even in death, we may serve our Queen and fuel her empire.
"
-
Transcendent Spirit
Viridian Jewel
"
Destined for sacrifice, they were dressed in garments that blurred the lines between this world and the next.
"
-
Shadowstitch
Sacrificial Garb
Trivia
When introduced on 1.1.0, GGG awarded the 50 first players who killed Atziri on Ambush and Invasion leagues,
with
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
or
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
respectively.
Version history
Version
Changes
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects, Betrayal targets, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
3.3.0
(undocumented) Introduced as a secret boss of
Throne of Atziri
of
The Temple of Atzoatl
1.1.0
Introduced to the game.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.9.0
(Not in patch notes) Now fixed at level 80, instead of varied with the map tier that Zana appears
3.8.2
Fixed a bug introduced in 3.8.0 where
Augmented Synthete'
s large projectile nova skill did not deal damage.
3.8.0
Introduced to the core game.
If you're lucky, Zana will now offer 5 new unique maps as part of her missions. Each map contains a boss encounter originally from the Synthesis league, and each can drop unique items originally exclusive to that league. Note, you cannot obtain Fractured items, nor will you have any access to the Synthesiser.
3.6.0
Introduced to the game as league-boss.

======================================================================
PAGE: Avatar of Thunder
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters, Map bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Avatar_of_Thunder
======================================================================

Avatar of Thunder
Avatar of Thunder
Location(s)
The Vinktar Square
Resistance(s)
Elemental
Chaos
Modifier(s)
Quick
Skill(s)
Default Attack
Cyclone
Summon Monsters
Earthquake
Living Bomb
VinktarHide
The Avatar of Thunder
is a boss residing in the unique map
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Location
To fight the Avatar of Thunder, one must defeat all four bosses from the map, located in each corner. The player will then have to go to the center of the map, and open the gate, to enter the boss arena.
Fight
Summary
To spawn the Avatar of thunder, the player will have to click the Staff holder in the middle of the arena. The fight will be a succession of 7 phases, during which the player will have to defeat each of the bosses he previously defeated during the map.
Phase One
During phase one, the Avatar of thunder will appear, dealing some lightning damage in the spawn area. He will then procede to cast a spell spawning 3 pillars of lightning that will move randomly (almost randomly, they seem to target the player), dealing a significant amount of damages to the player if he gets caught in their range. The beams can stack up, and will shock the player. The Avatar of thunder will also use a lightning version of
Earthquake
, lightning version of
Cyclone
, leaving shocked ground behind him, and a charging
lightning nova
.
Phase Two
The Avatar of thunder will warp out, and summon one of the four corner bosses that has been defeated previously. It can be any of the four,
Guardian of the North
,
Guardian of the East
,
Guardian of the South
or
Guardian of the West
.
Phase Three
Phase three will be similar to phase one, the avatar of thunder will come back and use the same set of skills that he used in phase one.
Phase Four
Phase Four will be similar to phase two, the avatar of thunder will warp out, and summon this time two of the unique bosses previously defeated.
Phase Five
Phase Five will be similar to phase one, the avatar of thunder will come back and use the same set of skills that he used in phase one.
Phase Six
Phase Six will be similar to phase two, the avatar of thunder will warp out, and summon this time three of the unique bosses previously defeated.
Phase Seven
Phase Seven will be similar to phase one, the avatar of thunder will come back and use the same set of skills that he used in phase one, but this time he will be killable, and will drop one of the
Vessel of Vinktar
.
Exclusive drops
The Avatar of Thunder always drops Vessel of Vinktar, which has 5 variants:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..

Version
Changes
2.1.0
Introduced to the game.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.5.0
The Atlas of Worlds has been shuffled. Most maps have changed tier and location within the Atlas.
Many map bosses have also shifted around. Some have been retired and replaced!
3.1.0
Added 32 new maps including two new Unique maps.
The entire Atlas has been reworked. Many map names have changed, as have their balance, tier and position on the Atlas. Many maps have also had their boss fights swapped or completely reworked.
The rules used to generate monsters in endgame maps have been significantly reworked and rebalanced around the rate at which we expect players to reliably clear out the map. In general, narrow, linear maps will contain fewer total monsters than maps with complex layouts. We will be closely monitoring and adjusting the balance of this change and expect to make further changes and improvements over time.

======================================================================
PAGE: Baran, the Crusader
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters, Map bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Baran,_the_Crusader
======================================================================

Baran, the Crusader
Baran, the Crusader
Location(s)
Crusader's Sanctum
Quest(s)
Picking Up the Pieces
Chasing a Dream
The Crusader
Resistance(s)
50%
Fire
50%
Cold
50%
Lightning
30%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
Skill(s)
Rune Detonation
Slam
Dash
"Triggered Delayed Blast"
"The Crusader" redirects here. For the quest, see
The Crusader (quest)
.
This page is about the boss monster.  For the notable passive skill, see
Crusader
.
Baran, the Crusader
is one of the Elderslayers / Conquerors of the Atlas. He serves as one of the end-game bosses of the Atlas. The boss fight itself has 8 different levels, based on the player's current Atlas Awakening level. It is colloquially known as
A1 Baran
through
A8 Baran
.
Items influenced by the Crusader have a red icon on its header
and have unique
Crusader modifiers
.
Non-influenced items can have the Crusader's influence added using a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, which is a rare drop from Baran.
Defeating Baran for the first item in a region will drop a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
that can be socketed into
Atlas citadels
. There are 8 atlas regions thus there are 8 Crimson Watchstones.
Encounter
Players must first spawn a Conqueror within the Atlas using the steps in the
Atlas quick guide
. Then talking to Kirac and Zana will open a portal to the Conqueror's citadel. Defeating the map boss will open a portal to the Conqueror's arena, where they must fight Baran.
Tips
Baran is notable for dealing a high amount of
Lightning
damage, and is also the only Conqueror to deal this type of damage.
Players need maintain constant mobility and avoid the Runes tiles and lightning shots, as they can be deadly.
Baran's attacks are very powerful, but have a slow and noticeable wind-up to them. Stay mobile before each attack and retaliate between attacks.
Baran's lightning attack where he fires three lightning shots in a small spread can easily
one-shot
most builds. Always stay moving during this part.
Baran's ground slam and rune cast have very long startup times, but make sure to keep your distance for the ground slam.
When Baran is channeling his runes, stand in the corners of the arena to maximize your safe zone area.
If Baran casts more runes when he already has some up, you can stand in the runes right before he creates more to overlap them.
At high levels, unless you have a very powerful build, it's highly recommended to
not
add increased speed to the map, It lets him spawn more runes and fire his deadliest attacks faster and leave him far less vulnerable.
Attacks
Melee with Directional Knockback
Default melee attack
Slam
Briefly charges up and slams his sceptre in front of him, dealing massive physical damage. The sceptre releases lightning projectiles in a spread pattern.
Deals 300% of Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage
+30 to Melee Strike Range
66% less Attack Speed
Deals 110% of Base Attack Damage
Dash To Target
Baran dashes to the player, dealing no damage.
Trigger Beam
Fires three bolts of lightning that hit in a small area, then explode. Damage is dealt both when first fired and on explosion. These bolts can overlap, and are
extremely deadly
to most builds.
Deals 2138 to 3207 Physical Damage
75% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage
Beam Impact
Fires multiple lightning bolts in a scattered pattern. Same as the previous attack, but much less deadly since they are much less likely to overlap.
Deals 969.2 to 2908 Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage
Triggered Delayed Blast
Deals 2102 to 3154 Physical Damage
75% of Physical Damage Converted to Lightning Damage
Rune Detonation
After a lengthy charge up, Baran creates Runes in your current location in a cross pattern.
They will be placed in squares of his arena. They last a long time before disappearing, and at higher levels Baran can have more than one cross pattern of Runes active.
Runes deal damage over time and degenerate mana. When stepped on, they will also detonate after a delay to deal lightning damage and
shock
the player.
Summon Daemon
Summons a minion that assists him in combat.
Video
Drops
Exclusive
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Crusader items
(items with the Crusader influence)
Awakened support skill gems
Read
list of awakened support skill gems
for full list
Lore
Zana
From
Zana
:
Like many of those once loyal to the Templar regime, Baran held particular contempt for his former brethren. Our mutual disdain for Dominus helped us bond. We stayed up far too late on many nights, discussing where science and spirituality overlapped... and where they clashed.
While I tended to agree with Veritania's secular view that people must be responsible for their own actions, Baran was still a firm believer that trust in God was necessary for good moral judgement. Despite everything the Templar put him through, his faith was unshaken.
We didn't always see eye-to-eye, but we respected each other's positions. Of course, once the madness set in, that respect vanished, replaced by fevered argument and name-calling. As the group dwindled, he found reasons to cast doubt on each of the departed. In our last fight before the two of us went our separate ways, he accused me of being sent by a shadowy demon to draw him away from the righteous path...
And then I was alone.
Conquerors of the Atlas
Baran is the younger brother of
Officer Kirac
. In
Picking Up the Pieces
, Kirac asked the player to investigate
The Templar Laboratory
to find out what Baran and
Zana
were doing. The player encountered a broken
map device
. Therefore, the protagonist and Kirac search for another map device in a vault.
Upon rebuilding a
map device
from spare parts, Kirac asks the player to search for Baran in Maps in the
Chasing a Dream
quest. The player instead finds
Zana
in an abandoned Citadel of a Map. Then the player finds Baran in the corner regions of the Atlas in
The Crusader
quest.
The player encounters Baran several times but in the first few, Baran escapes. In the final fight the player finally kills Baran, who then leads the player to
Sirus
, who is also killed by the player. After these events, Oriath is utterly obliterated by Sirus, and the Oriathians flee from there and take refuge on an island.
Echoes of the Atlas
The epilogue storyline in the
Echoes of the Atlas
expansion introduces
the Maven
as the ultimate endgame Atlas boss, in parallel to
Sirus
and the Conquerors. Kirac speculates that after the death of his brother Baran, the player was still able to encounter Baran due to the Maven resurrecting him.
This also addressed the long-standing plot hole of how the player could encounter and slay the Conquerors (and other bosses in map) repeatedly.
"
You saw Baran? How is that possible, if you slew him? Unless -
It's the Maven. It has to be. She's been recreating other powerful creatures of the Atlas for her own amusement. Of course she would do the same to my brother, given his notable strength. None of the creatures out there can challenge him, either, which means the Maven is resurrecting him to watch his fights with you, and the Atlas is already the perfect battleground for such a show.
Keep her entertained, Exile. It will give us more time to learn about her. If we can play this right, then perhaps Baran can be saved after all.
"
- Kirac
Baran's Journal
Main page:
Baran's journals
Baran's journals
are clickable lore items dropped each time you defeat Baran.
Dialogue
Upon defeating Baran in the highest difficulty (Awakening Level 8), he says:
Kirac...sent you? You must keep him out of the Atlas...I cannot be saved...he will find only his own doom, endlessly pursuing my rescue...he will end up...like me...
Tell Zana...she was right...to seal us away...
Sirus
must never...be...

, another Conqueror of the Atlas

, another Conqueror of the Atlas
Veritania, the Redeemer
, another Conqueror of the Atlas

======================================================================
PAGE: Breachlord
CATEGORIES: Unique monsters
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Breachlord
======================================================================

The
Breachlords
are five endgame bosses, located inside the five types of
Breaches
. Each Breachlord can be battled in its own domain, a map area only accessible by activating the corresponding
Breachstone
in the
map device
. Breachlords drop exclusive unique items. Killing them is the only way to acquire
Blessings
. When inside the Breachlord's domain, the player must fight their way through a breach while a timer counts down. Killing enemies will expand the timer. If the timer reaches zero, the stage ends and the player will be unable to encounter the Breachlord.
Breachlords
Xoph, Dark Embers
(level 70)
Esh, Forked Thought
(level 70)
Tul, Creeping Avalanche
(level 70)
Uul-Netol, Unburdened Flesh
(level 75)
Chayula, Who Dreamt
(level 80)
Version History
Version
Changes
2.5.0
Introduced to the game.

======================================================================
PAGE: Brutus, Lord Incarcerator
CATEGORIES: Act 1 unique monsters, Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Brutus,_Lord_Incarcerator
======================================================================

Brutus, Lord Incarcerator
Brutus, Lord Incarcerator
Location(s)
The Upper Prison
Quest(s)
The Caged Brute
Resistance(s)
Resists
Elemental Damage
Resists
Chaos Damage
Skill(s)
Ground Slam
Chain Pull Attack
Spiked Ground
Summon Cages
Brutus, Lord Incarcerator
, commonly just called Brutus, is a unique monster in the
Upper Prison
in
Act 1
.
Defeating him is the objective of the quest
The Caged Brute
.
A more powerful variant,
Reassembled Brutus
is encountered in
Act 6
as side-boss.
Lore
High above the crashing surf of Wraeclast, shrouded in the fog and ceaseless driving rain, stands an imposing structure. No one remembers who built it, and it is known among the island's inhabitants simply as "The Prison". Throughout the ages, as various tribes and societies have risen to power on Wraeclast, they have always locked their criminals deep inside The Prison's formidable stone walls. The ancient fortress keeps its prisoners and countless secrets locked far away. Sometimes, on cold, still nights, you can hear the tortured wailing of the doomed souls still trapped within its confines.
Every jail needs a warden, and The Prison is no exception. Though the job has been held by countless men over the ages, anyone living today can only recall one name: Brutus. He was the overseer of The Prison when Wraeclast's old men were just children, and he is the overseer to this day. Mothers get their children to behave by telling them that Brutus will come to see them. He is Wraeclast's Boogeyman.
Brutus ran The Prison with a legendary cruel streak. Under his stewardship, not a single soul escaped its soaring walls, and men were terrified at the prospect of a term inside. Alas, even great, terrible men cannot conquer the ravages of time, and as Brutus' years drew to a close, he became steadily more deranged and dangerous. He began to invite practitioners of dark, fel arts to The Prison to experiment on his captives, and eventually, in a desperate bid to extend his own waning life, he allowed them to experiment on him.
For days the halls of The Prison echoed with Brutus' awful shrieking, and when it was over, he was transformed into a hideous, hulking mutant. As the Necromancers crowded around to witness the results of their twisted endeavor, Brutus burst free from his bonds and descended on them with savagery. Their aged, frail bodies offered little resistance to his assault, and within moments the chamber was drenched in Necromancer blood. Brutus then tore through the entire level of the prison, leaving none alive in his merciless rampage.
When word of the slaughter reached the lower levels, the remaining guards made the only choice left to them. They barred the gates, locking the monster in the topmost level of The Prison. It did not take long for Brutus to realize he was trapped, and in his mad rage to escape, he pounded his mighty fists into a raw meaty pulp on the jail walls. So far, the gates have held, and Brutus remains sealed away, unable to inflict further carnage. Time has not diminished his anguish, and woe be unto anyone foolish enough to engage him in combat.
Strategy
This section needs to be updated
.
Please
update this article
to reflect newly available information.
Brutus hits are slow but hard. Pay attention to his movement, when he starts his ground slam attack, do your best to dodge it. He can also pull you in with his chain, and although this ability can hit his allies, it will not damage them. As the fight progresses Brutus starts using spike traps and calls for reinforcements from caged skeletons that are hanging from the ceiling.
But he doesn't have much health. A skill with damage over time, such as
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
will take care of him pretty easily.
Achievements
Killing Brutus grants you an achievement:
Name
Description
Mercy Killing
Kill Brutus, Warden of Axiom Prison.
Map Variants
Penitentiary Incarcerator
, a stronger and faster version of Brutus is featured in the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
. The spiked ground's area of effect has also been increased.
Text and Speech
Brutus is voiced by New Zealand actor,
Damien Caine
.
Encounter
Video

Old encounter
Version history
Version
Changes
3.15.0
We have rebalanced various monsters found throughout Act One to make them more dangerous. Note, the balance changes made to Act One Bosses and monsters also apply to their Endgame Map versions.
Monster life has been increased by roughly 46% at level 1, tapering down to the same amount of life at level 84. Some bosses and monsters in Act 1, such as Hillock and Oozeback have had their life reduced to partially counteract this change.
Brutus
has taken the time to renovate his arena.
3.7.0
Improved the early-game experience. Fights in the first three Acts will be tougher but more responsive, involved and dynamic. This includes fights against basic monsters!
1.2.0
The Brutus and Merveil boss fights have been redesigned. These changes also affect their end-game Map equivalents.
Brutus' hook now only pulls the target that he was aiming at.
1.0.0
Item drops from Merveil, Brutus, Vaal Oversoul, Piety and Dominus have been substantially increased in Cruel and Merciless difficulties.
0.11.0d
Brutus and Kole have had their damage reduced.
0.9.13c
Fixed a server crash that could occur when killing Brutus or Merveil.
0.9.13
The Merveil, Brutus and Vaal Oversoul fights now lock the door behind you for the duration of the fight, so that players aren't able to retreat to the linked area. Portals are still allowed.
0.9.12r
Fixed a client crash that could occur when fighting Brutus.
0.9.12qq
Added a hook attack to Brutus.
0.9.11
Increased the experience that Brutus yields when killed.
0.9.7
Brutus now explodes shortly after being slain.
0.9.3e
Increased the damage of Brutus' Ground Slam.
0.9.3b
Increased the Life and damage of various bosses: Brutus, Merveil (both forms), the Deep Dweller (both forms) and the Hideous Abomination
0.8.8
Fixed a bug that could lead to Brutus spawning on other prison levels.

Lore
Cameria is the member of
Immortal Syndicate
. However, according to
Kirac
's "
Helena
" dialogue, Cameria is a psychotic disciple of
Gravicius
. It indicated that Cameria may be a former member of
Templar
Blackguards.
The noun "Cameria" first appears in-game as
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, a supporter-designed unique. "Cameria's Maul" is in fact a pun of
Mall of America
. It is not certain that the name of the monster is affected by the supporter or not.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
Several veiled modifiers now have fewer tiers. Low tiers contribute more towards unlocking higher tiers, however it will, on average, take more unveils to unlock the highest tier.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.
References
See also

, fated variant of Cameria's Maul.

Catarina, Master of Undeath
Catarina, Master of Undeath
Level(s)
68-83
Location(s)
Mastermind's Lair

1 211 796

1426

1.35

30%

30%

30%
Lightning
15%
Chaos

80% Less Curse Effectiveness
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
Immune To Knockback
Unwavering
Catarina, Master of Undeath
is the leader of the
Immortal Syndicate
. She resides in the
Mastermind's Lair
.
Skills
Carpet Mortar
Deals 931 to Physical Damage
25% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Skills fire an additional Projectile
X Circle
Deals 931 to 1396 Physical Damage
Soul Mortar
Deals 620.7 to 931 Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Skills fire an additional Projectile
Donut of Destiny
Deals 67.41 to 101.1 Physical Damage
Volatile Object Explode
Deals 387.9 to 581.9 Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Rollout
Deals 400% of Damage
25% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Sideways Carpet Bomb
Deals 633.9 to 950.8 Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Fight
Jun, Veiled Master
will open up a portal to the
Mastermind's Lair
where you will meet Catarina, Master of Undeath. The zone level will match the highest level Safehouse you have completed.
The Forbidden Vault
The fight starts off with basic
attacks
. Once she has taken some damage, she summons unique
zombies
to aid her. Once she has lost enough life, she will open a portal and flee to her safe house.
Zombies
Xian Song
etc.
The Unearthed Hideout
Hunt the Mastermind through the hordes of monsters with Jun to the The Sculpture Garden.
The Sculpture Garden
The arena is a square with four large
totems
in the corners and a sculpture in the center. Catarina will continue to use her attacks from the Forbidden Vault; after she reaches a certain amount of
life
she will turn to the center statue and heal herself, simultaneously releasing one of the the syndicate leaders to attack and distract you.
Once the leader has been killed she will move to the Blackflame phase where the statue in the middle also casts
spells
in a circular pattern. She will also start to charge up one of the totems in the corner which will make her immune to damage when the totem is ready. Attack her meanwhile until a marker beneath you appears, go to the totem and it will absorb your soul until it breaks. The totem releases a cloud that
damages over time
upon destruction. When the totem is destroyed the cycle repeats itself. All totems have to be destroyed to defeat Catarina.
At low life, she will start to summon a pack of volatile
skeletons
. Walk over them before they spawn to destroy them. If left alive, they will constantly chase the player slowly to explode on you.
There's not a lot of monsters in this fight so there might be difficulties getting
flasks
recharged.
Safehouse
The safehouse contains a reward room for each Syndicate member, with two of each of the appropriate rewards from each division leader, at one rank higher. If the Syndicate member was at tier 3, this will upgrade their rewards to tier 4.
Boss drops
Catarina, Master of Undeath will always drop
scarabs
and one of the following
unique items
:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Signature mod
Unveil only
:
+2 to Level of Socketed Support Gems
+(5-8)% to Quality of Socketed Support Gems

of

Legacy mods
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
One of the legacy mods,
+1 to Level of Socketed Support Gems
, can be identified by its required level = 1.
The required levels of the current generation of crafted mod (introduced in
version 3.6.0
, are 48 (60*0.8) and 64 (80*0.8) respectively, which means the +2 version is visually the same, only at the back end the mod id are different.
Video
See also
Lifegiver's Manifesto
Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
We have improved the Betrayal reward system so that slaying the Mastermind is more worthwhile. Now, if the Mastermind is slain, her Immortal Syndicate Members drop their rewards at one tier higher than their current tier. This change introduces a fourth tier of reward from each member, and gives more reason to use a larger variety of Syndicate targets and safehouse leaders. Some of the best existing rewards have been moved to the fourth tier. We've also improved some of the less-interesting or less-valuable rewards.
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
Added
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
to item drop pool.
3.10.0
The Mastermind can now drop Metamorph Scarabs.
3.9.0
The Mastermind will now drop Scarabs when slain.
3.8.0
Upon completing the Mastermind encounter, you'll now have access to a reward room for every single Immortal Syndicate member currently with a job on your board (including prisoners). That's essentially up to 14 rooms worth of rewards, plus the Mastermind themselves.
Added a Stash to the Mastermind's Safehouse reward area because, well, you're going to need it.
Mastermind Intelligence no longer decays.
Upon defeating the Mastermind, the Betrayal board state is reset to a similar number of members and relationships as the starting state. However, Safehouse Intelligence is no longer reset upon defeating the Mastermind.
Mastermind's Safehouse now has 200% increased Experience from monsters.
3.6.0
The Mastermind encounter has been simplified, clarified, and made easier:
The Mastermind's Pillar Surges now activate at a slower rate.
The Mastermind's Volatile Skeletons will no longer spawn in graveyard areas with an active green fire effect.
Increased the radius at which the Mastermind's Volatile Skeletons explode (making it easier to safely trigger them).
Improved the Mastermind's behaviour in and around the active green fire graveyard area thing places. She shouldn't get stuck in corners anymore!
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.10.0
The "
Citaqualotl's"
one-handed weapon modifiers now grant 50 to 66% increased damage dealt by your minions (from 70 to 74%). The two-handed weapon versions now grant 85 to 94% increased damage dealt by your minions (from 105 to 110%).
You can use a Divine Orb on existing weapons if you're sick of how strong your minion build is.
Chests in the present-day Incursion Temple that previously contained either an Incursion Unique or a magic item with an Architect's mod now also drop 2 to 3 rare items with Architect's mods.
3.5.0
Introduced to the core game
3.3.0
Introduced to the game as league mini-boss.
Notes

Crackling Essence
is one of the side boss in the
The Lord's Labyrinth
boss room. Since the layout rotates daily, this monster may or may not present in the layout of that day.
The monster will try to merge with
Izaro
in its first appearance, which grant the boss a buff that last for whole Labyrinth. In the final boss room, the golem will re-appear and uses its attacks.
It is a
lightning golem
, a subtype of
golem
.

Craiceann, First of the Deep
is a unique boss located in
Craiceann's Cove
. It is one of the four
Spirit Beasts
. According to in-game
Bestiary
, it is a
Lurcher
.
Portals to Craiceann's Cove can be crafted via a
beastcrafting
recipe using the
Craicic Spider Crab
and any three yellow beasts. Note that buying the Craicic Spider Crab beast from other players and adding it to your bestiary WILL NOT unlock the portal recipe and grant access to Craiceann's Cove. Player need to unlock the recipe prior. However, beastcrafting is account-bound which meant it can be done in previous league.
Abilities
Strategy
Exclusive drops
Craiceann will always drop one of the following:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
The boss will also be captured by Einhar, unlocking the
Aspect of the Crab
Beastcraft
(add
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods.name"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
suffix to a non-unique item). The captured boss is an ingredient for this recipe.
Note that fighting this beast boss again at the blood altar in the Menagerie will not drop another unique item.

PAGE: Daresso, King of Swords

======================================================================

Daresso, King of Swords
Daresso, King of Swords
Location(s)
The Grand Arena
Quest(s)
The King of Desire
Resistance(s)
Resists
Elemental Damage
Resists
Chaos Damage
Skill(s)
Cold wave
Attack Combo
Summon Ice Clones
Charge
Floating Swords
Enrage
Daresso, King of Swords
is a unique monster located in
The Grand Arena
.
He is the final enemy in this area and defeating him during the quest
The King of Desire
will cause him to drop
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Lore
Daresso was the Sword King, a title reserved to the most exalted duelist of the Grand Arena of Theopolis. An orphaned slave boy, at age 13, he was forced into fighting in
the pits
for the amusement of gamblers. Daresso had a knack for fighting; at age 15, he made his first kill on another man, and eventually went on to become a duelist at the Grand Arena.
At first, Daresso fought simply to survive. Daresso would eventually become outmatched, but just before he accepted defeat, he had first laid his eyes upon
Merveil
. With newfound purpose, Daresso turned the fight around and began fighting for his new desire: the favor of Merveil. He rose up in rank even higher and eventually challenged the Sword King. Following his victory against the Sword King, Daresso immediately asked for Merveil's hand in marriage.
Daresso proposed to Merveil with the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, unaware of its curse. Seeing his beloved slowly turn into a monster, Daresso slipped away into Wraeclast, hoping to find a cure for her curse. Although the details afterwards are unknown, Daresso eventually perished in Wraeclast.
Dialogues
"
I am Daresso, the Sword King. It was here, in the pits of Theopolis that I made my name, killing rabid dogs and wild boars before I could even be considered a man. In this so glorious of arenas, I care nothing for your past, only your future... and for that, you will have to fight!
"
- Daresso -
Daresso's Dream
intro
"
People of the gutters, steel your bellies, for the slaughter is at hand!
"
- Daresso -
Daresso's Dream
boss room
"
An adequate performance, challenger, if a little messy. Yet
Barkhul
and his stinking flock were simply an audition. Let us see how you fare in the Grand Arena of Theopolis!
"
- Daresso -
Daresso's Dream
boss room, after the player cleared all the monster
"
Welcome to the Grand Arena of Theopolis. It is here that I first laid eyes upon my true purpose, my lady Merveil. It is our inspiration that defines us. What do you fight for, Gladiator? Hundreds of warriors tried to lay claim to the same title as I, but I defeated them all. Victory goes not to the able, but to the ambitious.
"
- Daresso -
The Grand Arena
intro
"
A battle worthy of song! The way you fight reminds me of how it feels to be possessed with the ideal of victory. Is your passion enough to claim a crown? We shall see.
"
- Daresso -
The Grand Arena
when the player walked towards to the boss room
There are additional monologues from the plaques in
The Grand Arena
.
Abilities
Ice Clones
- An attack skill that spawns projectiles from locations around the player with a short delay. The projectiles look like icy clones of Daresso, but are regular attack projectiles in all respects.
Attack Combo
- A single target melee attack skill that does three sequential hits. The final hit has a percentage of physical damage added as extra cold damage. This attack skips evasion and dodge checks because of a bug but it can still be blocked.
Cold Waves
- Similar to
Reave
skill, it does three waves per use with increased radius for each successive wave. 100% of the physical damage of this attack is converted to cold, and the skill has Resolute Technique (always hit, never crit).
Enrage
- Activates automatically each time a quarter of his life is removed. It grants him a buff increasing attack speed that lasts a short duration, except for the last time he uses it, in which case the duration is infinite.  While kneeling, attacking him will restore health to him, up to the nearest 25% (25%, 50%, 75%).
Charge
- Pretty much the same as
Whirling Blades
skill.
Sword Clouds
- This is an environmental effect, meaning it isn't directly used by Daresso. The cloud is a physical damage spell that repeatedly hits everything in its area of effect, it cannot cause stun.
Video

======================================================================
PAGE: Death Blossom
CATEGORIES: Talisman bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Death_Blossom

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "COUNT(DISTINCT events._pageName)"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Talismans are infused with the power of the First Ones, the primeval gods of the ancient Ezomytes. The creatures of Wraeclast are instinctively drawn to these relics and the feral vigour they impart.
Description on the official Path of Exile website
The
Talisman leagues
were a pair of
challenge leagues
, one standard and the other hardcore. In these leagues, monsters are possessed by ancient Ezomyte
Talismans
. These monsters are indicated by green Talisman symbols over their heads. The Talismans grant them additional strength and properties in battle. Killing one of these monsters allows the player to claim its Talisman for himself.
The standard and hardcore versions have the same core mechanics but slightly different items available.
The leagues started on December 11, 2015 along with the release of
Version 2.1.0
and ended March 4, 2016.
Characters that died in standard Talisman league respawned in the standard Talisman league, while those that died in hardcore Talisman league respawned in
Standard league
. At the end of the Talisman leagues, all characters and
stashes
were moved to Standard league and
Hardcore league
.
Modifier
All areas had:
Ancient Talismans possess monsters throughout Wraeclast
Items
Talismans
Main page:
Talisman
Talismans are a type of
amulet
. All talismans are
corrupted
when dropped, so they cannot be modified. They have special implicit modifiers more powerful than ordinary amulets and can be normal, magic, rare, or unique. For the full list of talismans possession effects and other related information, see
Talisman
.
Shared between HC and SC
These base items were exclusive to the standard and hardcore Talisman leagues:

Talisman
article for details.

Defiled Proclamation
is a unique Stone Golem located in
The Western Forest (Act 6)
. It can be encountered by finding a circle of stone pillars  and interacting with the rubble on the ground labelled Defiled Proclamation.  After interacting with the rubble Defiled Proclamation will spawn.  A
Doedre's Torment
will also spawn between each of the stone pillars.  Like the
Doedre Darktongue
boss fight three partially overlapping circles will appear that will curse the player if they stand in a circle.  If the player is standing in multiple circles, they will be inflicted with multiple curses.  These circles will disappear after roughly 10 seconds.
The purple circle contains a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
effect
The green circle contains an
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
effect
The red circle contains a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
effect
The circle of stone pillars.

Doedre the Defiler
Doedre the Defiler

40% Fire Resistance

33% reduced curse effectiveness
cannot be stunned for 2s after stun finished
etc.
Doedre the Defiler
is the map boss of
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
. She is based on the
Doedre, Darksoul
boss of Act 9.
In a tunnel leading to the boss room are Doedre's Monoliths that apply curses in a way similar to those applied by
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
. Each monolith applies one of
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
(green),
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
(purple), or
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
(red).
In the boss room, Doedre summons up to 3 Accursed Monoliths, which apply debuffs to the player character and their allies. These debuffs are known as
Doedre's Withering
(green),
Doedre's Suffering
(purple) and
Doedre's Torment
(red).

======================================================================
PAGE: Doedre the Vile
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Doedre_the_Vile
======================================================================

Doedre the Vile
Doedre the Vile
Location(s)
Doedre's Cesspool
Quest(s)
Essence of the Hag
Doedre the Vile
is a unique
monster
located in
Doedre's Cesspool
in
Act 8
. It is the second
Doedre
encounter during the whole storyline.
The map variant is
Portentia, the Foul
(
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
).
Strategy
This second Doedre encounter takes place in
Doedre's Cesspool
at the end of the Act 8 quest
Essence of the Hag
. A valve in the center of the map allows the player to cycle through the 3 stages (Red, Purple, and Green) of the fight, each which applies a
debuff
on the player and effects the boss' behavior. On activating the valve, Doedre leaves the player alone for a few seconds.
Buffs and Debuffs
See also:
Effluent
and
Sewage Toxins
The following is primarily from
Huizui'
s video (linked below) but in a written format; check out his video if you prefer a visual guide.
Buff Color
Buff Description (boss buff)
Red
Doedre takes reduced damage
Green
Doedre Deals more damage
Purple
Doedre becomes sonic: she moves, attacks, and casts faster
Arena Color
Effluent Description (player debuff)
Red
Random spawning bubbles that pop will apply a debuff which makes you take 10% increased damage, stacking up to 10 times
(Min: 10%, Max: 100% damage taken)
Green
Random spawning mobs, which apply Green Effluent debuff on death that makes you deal 7% less damage, stacking up to 10 times
(Min: 7%, Max: 70% damage dealt)
Purple
Random spawning purple clouds will apply chaos degen and debuff, which makes you 7% slower, stacking up to 10 times
(Min: 7%, Max: 70% slowed)
Abilities
Attacks
Attacks Description
Warp
Doedre teleports; this is similar to
Lightning Warp
but in a red bubble size instead of a sign on the floor
Wand Strike
Throws out 3 projectiles (pierces you)
Bubble Burst
Throws a projectile that becomes multiple and into bursts in a small area
Unlike several bosses previously encountered, all of Doedre attacks deal physical damage exclusively; therefore, one of the safest ways to defeat her is to capitalize on defenses that mitigate this kind of damage such as Armor, Endurance Charges, Fortify and other sources of flat reduction while engaging her during the Red or Green phases - it's also worth noting that the debuff of the latter can be completely circumvented by simply not killing her spawned minions.
Quest
Essence of the Hag
Video

======================================================================
PAGE: Dominus
CATEGORIES: Act 3 unique monsters, Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Dominus
======================================================================

Dominus
Dominus
Level(s)
36
Location(s)
The Upper Sceptre of God
Quest(s)
Sceptre of God
Resistance(s)
Resists
Elemental Damage
Resists
Chaos Damage
Skill(s)
As Dominus, High Templar:
Lightning Stream
Lightning Fist
Chilling Bond
Ground Lightning teleport
Mark of Dominus
As Dominus, Ascendant:
Rain of Blood
Charge
Grab
Cleave
Blood Projectiles
Dominus, High Templar
is the final boss of
act 3
, located in
The Upper Sceptre of God
, on the
Tower Rooftop
(after climbing 3 or 4 staircases in the Upper Sceptre of God) Upon defeating him,
Lady Dialla
appears to congratulate the player and opens the way to
act 4
.
Lore
He is the High Templar, the highest rank of the
order of the Templar
, which appear to be the rulers of the theocracy of
Oriath
. Dominus also controls the
Ebony Legion
. His name is Latin for "Lord" or "Master".
Abilities
Dominus, High Templar
unofficial Skill name
Effect
Lightning stream (id:
DominusLightningSouls
)
Shoots out a straight line of rapid-fire projectiles that deal lightning damage as they pass through the player.
Lightning fist (id:
DominusLightningSlam
)
A slow powerful strike to the ground dealing AoE lightning and physical damage.
Chilling Bond
Casts multiple totems linked to each other with
Cold Beam
that chill and deal cold damage over time.
Ground lightning teleport (id:
DominusDash
)
Teleports to the player, leaving behind a wide streak of shocking ground between the start and end locations.
Summoning
Summons melee miscreations from the devices.
Mark of Dominus
Inflicts the player with "Mark of Dominus" directing miscreations to chase the player and explode.
Dominus, Ascendant
Skill
Effect
Rain of Blood
A rain of blood washes on the area, causing players to receive stacks of corrupted blood approximately at the rate of one stack per second. Dominus also casts a large stationary shield around him that protects from the rain.
Melee
A regular melee attack.
Charge
A fast charging move towards the target with a heavy blow at the end.
Grab
When moving far away from Dominus, a demonic hand emerges from the ground and attempts to grab the player and teleport him back to Dominus.
Cleave
Cleaves the target adding 2 stacks of corrupted blood per hit.
Blood Projectiles
Shoots a volley of gory/blood projectiles in the air that land dealing physical(?) AoE damage.
Summoning
Summons melee and ranged miscreations from the portals on the ground.
Strategy
The Dominus fight can be a tough and complicated encounter, and it is important to have capped
resistances
, most importantly lightning resistance. Instant-healing (seething/bubbling) life potions, resistance flasks and granite/jade flasks are extremely helpful during this fight. There are various stages to this encounter, each of which has different mechanics and strategies. If you are playing on a hardcore league, it is advisable to bring
Portal Scrolls
to this fight to replenish your potions during the fight, and to allow a quick escape should that be necessary.
Minibosses
Initially, Dominus himself will not fight. Instead, he will send three unique Legionnaires to fight for him:
Imperator Stantinus Bitterblade
(
Cyclone
, Ice Trail)
Draconarius Wilhelm Flamebrand
(Burning
Lightning Strike
,
Leap Slam
)
Compulsor Octavia Sparkfist
(
Spark
Totem,
Lightning Warp
)
During this period, regular legionnaires will also enter the fray alongside the uniques, until all three uniques are dealt with.
This phase should not be too difficult if the player isolates the uniques from each other and deals with them one by one. None of the uniques is particularly formidable on their own.
After these three bosses are defeated, the next three minibosses will enter the arena. Each one is a unique Miscreation, similar to the ones found in
The Lunaris Temple
:
Alal the Terrifying
(
Viper Strike
,
Ethereal Knives
)
Kali the Crazed
(Extra Projectiles Tentacle Miscreation)
Orcus the Reaver
(
Reave
,
Smoke Mine
)
Combined, these three uniques might pose a threat, but again, isolating them and dealing with them one by one should allow the player to complete this phase with minimal risk.
The First Phase - Human Dominus
Dominus' first, human form.
After all minibosses have been defeated, Dominus will descend to the arena and fight alongside the miscreations from the previous phase which will continue spawning until the very end of the fight. If the miscreations annoy you, you can lure Dominus to a relatively quiet spot on the roof and deal with him there, though he will still move around a bit, possibly causing you to attract some of the miscreations anyway. He has both a sizable life pool and a comparatively small energy shield. He has various attacks, each of which is quite dangerous in its own right and can be anticipated through various things Dominus says before using them. The attacks are:
The
debuff
Mark of Dominus
which forces dominus minions to target the debuffed player aggressively and explode when near the vicinity of the player.
An area-of-effect Lightning/physical-based spell (
Lightning Fist
) dealing very heavy damage. To counter this spell, either run away or make sure you have good lightning resistance, armor and a decent life/ES pool.
Granite
and
Topaz
flasks, preferably with the "of Grounding" suffix, is immensely helpful against this attack, but must be used before he initiates the attack, as damage dealt is calculated when it is initiated (similar to Vaal's smash), ignoring any resistances gained after the attack has initiated.
A rapid-firing attack (
Lightning Stream
) which appears to be evadable/blockable, but is still very dangerous. This can be dodged without too much difficulty by walking perpendicular to its line of fire.
A
cold damage
dealing
Searing Bond
-like cluster of totems which deal damage over time. These do not deal a lot of damage and are easy to avoid.
A
Flicker Strike
-like attack which Dominus uses to move around the arena.
The Second Phase - Dominus Ascendant
Dominus' second form.
Once Dominus has lost all his life on the first phase, he will move to the center of the arena and turn into a bloodred insect-like being called Dominus, Ascendant. During this phase, he will have no energy shield left, but still a sizable life pool. It is important to stay close to him during this phase due to the nature of his attacks during this phase:
A melee attack dealing sizable, but not incredibly high damage which appears to be hybrid physical/fire. You will probably have to tank this.
A rain of blood in the entire arena except for a bubble around Dominus himself. You will want to stay inside the bubble or risk losing a lot of HP very rapidly. This is the most dangerous attack during the second phase and it is not advisable to risk getting caught up in it, so stay close to him at all times during this phase.
Video
Achievements

Droolmaw
Droolmaw
Location(s)
The Belly of the Beast (Act 9)
Droolmaw
is a
unique monster
located in
The Belly of the Beast (Act 9)
. There is a 4-way intersection in The Belly of the Beast map. One branch is the direction the player came from, two branches with be a quick dead-end. The last branch leads to Droolmaw. It spawns in an otherwise empty room; however the passage to this room sometimes is blocked and you'll need to re-enter the map as a new instance.

======================================================================
PAGE: Drox, the Warlord
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters, Map bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Drox,_the_Warlord
======================================================================

Drox, The Warlord
Drox, The Warlord
Location(s)
Warlords's Keep
Resistance(s)
50%
Fire
50%
Cold
50%
Lightning
30%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
Drox, the Warlord
is one of the Elderslayers / Conquerors of the Atlas. He serves as one of the end-game bosses of the Atlas. The boss fight itself has 8 different levels, based on the player's current Atlas Awakening level. It is colloquially known as
A1 Drox
through
A8 Drox
.
Items influenced by the Warlord have a yellow icon on its header
and have unique
Warlord modifiers
.
Non-influenced items can have the Warlord's influence added using a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, which is a rare drop from Drox.
Defeating Al-Hezmin for the first item in a region will drop a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
that can be socketed into
Atlas citadels
. There are 8 atlas regions thus there are 8 Golden Watchstones.
Encounter
Players must first spawn a Conqueror within the Atlas using the steps in the
Atlas quick guide
. Then talking to Kirac and Zana will open a portal to the Conqueror's citadel. Defeating the map boss will open a portal to the Conqueror's arena, where they must fight Drox.
The arena is a cross-shaped area.
Tips
Drox often casts a war banner that increases his defense and the defense of nearby monsters.
Players can destroy the banners to reduce the difficulty of the fight.
Monsters often enter the area from the 4 edges of the arena.
Players must also keep moving to avoid the powerful
Glove Slam Impact
attack, wherein a giant glove appears above the player's position and slams the ground dealing high amounts of Physical damage.
Constant mobility during the fight is recommended.
Attacks
Default Attack - Strike your foes down with a powerful blow.
Cascade
Rubble Slam
Summon Banner
Melee - 25% less Attack Speed
Glove Slam Impact - Deals 190% of Base Attack Damage
Spawn Slam Glove
Double Hands - Deals 70% of Base Attack Damage
Items found from this monster
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
The boss always drop
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
if the player haven't obtain the watchstone of that region. The boss has a chance to drop
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
,
Warlord influence item
and
Awakened Support Skill Gems
(Read
List of awakened support skill gems
for a full list of gems)
Before 3.14, the boss also has a chance to drop unique items that exclusive to the 4 lesser
Elderslayers
. However, apparently most or all drop are moved to core drop table.
Lore
Main page:
Drox's journals
Drox's journals are clickable lore items dropped each time you defeat Drox.
From
Zana
:
I've seen my fair share of combat, Exile, but never have I seen someone more comfortable on the battlefield than Drox. Though he was not our leader, he was our commander. When an impossible decision had to be made in the midst of battle, somehow, Drox always found the right path forward. He led us through numerous situations we thought fatal with an unflagging warm grin that let us know he believed in us.
But at some point, almost imperceptibly, Drox stopped making decisions for the group, and started focusing only on his new dream. His smiles turned to scowls. He remained fixated on this mad idea of building a kingdom in the Atlas. He became cold to Veritania and distant to the rest of us. All his efforts went towards securing the Atlas and establishing law. His obsession was putting us in danger, and that is something I could not tolerate.
When I led the group away from him, he either didn't notice, or didn't care.
See also
List of warlord modifiers
Sirus, Awakener of Worlds
Al-Hezmin, the Hunter
Baran, the Crusader
Veritania, the Redeemer

Changes

added to the game.

The banners spawned by
the Warlord'
s Influence no longer grant damage immunity to surrounding monsters, they instead just provide reduced damage taken.

the Warlord'
s Influence could affect other banners.

The
Elder Guardians
are four map bosses from the
War for the Atlas
storyline, who each drop a different
map fragment
that is required to fight
the Elder
. They are the guardians of the The Elder.

Mechanics
A Elder-influenced map of Tier 14 or higher can be occupied by one of the Guardians. The map boss is replaced with the Guardian. Approaching the map boss will spawn a portal leading to the Guardian's arena, chosen at random out of three possible arenas for each Guardian. They will be affected by the map's modifiers.
The boss fight is split into three phases. At 2/3 and 1/3 health remaining, the arena switches locations. Each arena contains different stage hazards.
Upon slaying the boss, they will drop a
Map fragment
. Open a complete set of fragments in the map device can open portals to fight
the Elder
.
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
