Lore
Elder Guardians are the side-bosses that aid
The Elder
, akin to
Shaper Guardian
. During
War for the Atlas
and
Betrayal
expansions, in order to beat
Uber Elder
, the player need to spread Elder influence to Shaper Guardian Maps (which at that time on the
Atlas
at the centre) first to convert them to Elder Guardian Maps (the base map is still the Shaper Guardian Map) and beat all 4 of them to convert
The Shaper's Realm
into Uber Elder variant of the area.
However, due to the introduction of
Conquerors of the Atlas
expansion, such end-game lore is rewritten by the game developer. Instead of the player as the protagonist to beat the Elder, Shaper and their guardians , another group of NPC /
exiles
, known as
Elderslayers
/ Conquerors beat the Elder instead. Elderslayers themselves also turn into new villain in the expansion. To fill the plot hole that how the player can still enter and play the Elder Guardian Map (Shaper Guardian Map as well), new
Zana
voiceline was added:
"
You must think me a fool for taking so long to understand just how mad the Elderslayers have become, but... you don't know what we were up against. Look, I'm going to show you a memory of a battle against one of the Elder's guardians. Perhaps then you'll see why it wasn't so clear.
"
- Zana
The next annual expansion,
Echoes of the Atlas
, add
the Maven
as new end-game boss, which she has the ability to clone bosses to her
crucible
. A new atlas region,
The Uncharted Realms
, was added to the game as well. Elder Guardians is the objective of
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
These new lore explain the in-game reappearance of Elder Guardian as map boss, which the atlas and the Maven has ability to clone it.
See also
Shaper Guardians
Version history
Version
Changes
3.9.0e
Fixed a bug where Zana missions could offer
Elder Guardian Maps
earlier than intended. The minimum tier before these maps are offered by Zana missions is now 14.
3.9.0
The Atlas of Worlds has been reworked. You now start at the centre and will work your way outwards as you explore a new storyline.
The Shaper and Elder storyline is gone and has been replaced by a story that takes place in the aftermath of the old story.
Zana can now sell Shaper- and Elder-influenced maps once you have progressed your Atlas enough. These act like the old Atlas influence, creating new monsters, threats, and rewards.
The Elder, Shaper, and combined Elder and Shaper encounters can still be accessed. If you're lucky, Zana may offer a Shaper or
Elder guardian'
s map!
Elder Guardians are no longer in a separate instance to that of their map.
3.5.0
Items exclusive to the Shaper's guardians and the Elder's guardians now drop less frequently. Uniques exclusive to the Elder's guardians now also drop based on map tier, with some items unable to drop until higher map tiers.
(not in patch notes) Elder guardians now have a shared pool of exclusive drop, rather than tied to individual guardians.
3.1.0
Introduced to the game.
References

Signature mod
Elreon's signature mods are:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Elreon's mod cannot be crafted together with
Xopec's
mana cost reduction mod,
even they are in different mod groups according to the datamined values. Those mods have the same tag "has_mana_cost_mod". Players can obtain legacy item that have both mana cost reduction mods in permanent leagues by trading with other players.
However, Elreon's mana cost reduction mod can be crafted on other Xopec's mod that did not involve mana cost reduction, such as
increased maximum Mana
and
Mana gained for each Enemy hit by your Attacks
hybrid mod.
The Elreon's crafted mods also have a legacy variant
-# to Total Mana Cost of Skills
that affects channelling and non-channelling skills. The legacy item can only be obtainable in permanent leagues by trading with other players. Since all Elreon's mod are in the same mod group, players cannot craft both channelling and non-channelling signature mods on the same item.

old Elreon's

Version history
Version
Changes
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.8.0
Elreon's signature veil mod: the mana cost reduction crafted modifier has been split into two separate modifiers -- one for Channeling skill mana costs, and one for non-Channeling skill mana costs. Each has three ranks.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
In 3.6.0, Jun is introduced in Highgate in Act 9.
Thus, Elreon now can be encountered from Act 9 onwards only.
3.5.0
Elreon, Light's Judge
, is introduced to the game.
Elreon, Loremaster
is no longer Forsaken Masters.
References

Ersi, Mother of Thorns
Ersi, Mother of Thorns
Location(s)
The Sacred Grove
Resistance(s)
Resists elemental damage
Resists chaos damage
Modifier(s)
Reveal a random crafting effect that locks a random modifier on an item when Harvested
Ersi, Mother of Thorns
is one of the T4
Harvest Seed
bosses. it is the boss of Wild Seeds. Its seed,
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, is pre-planted in
The Sacred Grove
by random.
Upon killing all the monsters of the plot that contains Ersi, Mother of Thorns, it will grant a one-time-use harvest recipe.
The monster itself may also drop exclusive unique items.
Recipes
Reveal a random crafting effect that locks a random modifier on an item when Harvested
Fracture
a random modifier on an item with at least 5 modifiers, locking it in place. This can't be used on
Influenced
,
Synthesised
, or Fractured items
Fracture a Suffix on an item with least 3 Suffixes. This can't be used on Influenced, Synthesised, or Fractured items
Fracture a Prefix on an item with at least 3 Prefixes. This can't be used on Influenced, Synthesised, or Fractured items.
Abilities

======================================================================
PAGE: Farrul, First of the Plains
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Farrul,_First_of_the_Plains
======================================================================

Farrul, First of the Plains
Farrul, First of the Plains
Level(s)
80
Location(s)
Farrul's Den
Resistance(s)
Resists Elemental Damage
Resists Chaos Damage
Farrul, First of the Plains
is a unique boss located in
Farrul's Den
. It is one of the four
Spirit Beasts
.
Portals to Farrul's Den can be crafted via a
beastcrafting
recipe using the
Farric Tiger Alpha
and any three yellow beasts.
Note that buying the Farric Tiger Alpha beast from other players and adding it to your bestiary WILL NOT unlock the portal recipe and grant access to Farrul's Den. Player need to unlock the recipe prior, by capturing the monster in the wild. However, beastcrafting recipe is account-bound which meant it can be done in previous leagues.
Items found from this monster
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
When defeated, the boss will always drop one of the above items. Einhar will also capture the boss, unlocking the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "skill._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
beastcrafting
recipe (add
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods.name"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
suffix to a non-unique item). The captured boss is an ingredient for this recipe.
Note that fighting this beast boss again at the blood altar in the Menagerie will not drop another unique item.
Version history
Version
Changes
3.2.0
Added to the game.

PAGE: Fenumus, First of the Night
CATEGORIES: Unique monsters
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Fenumus,_First_of_the_Night
======================================================================

Fenumus, First of the Night
Fenumus, First of the Night
Level(s)
77
Location(s)
Fenumus' Lair
Health
7.3M
Damage
Physical
Chaos
Resistance(s)
Resists
Elemental Damage
Resists
Chaos Damage
Resists
Curses
Resists
Slows
Skill(s)
Spit Mortar
Summon Spiders
Hallucinate
Tail Spike
Tail Spike Swing
Ground Stab
Fenumus, First of the Night
is a
unique boss
located in
Fenumus' Lair
. It is one of the four
Spirit Beasts
.
Portals
to Fenumus' Lair can be crafted via a
beastcrafting
recipe using the
Fenumal Hybrid Arachnid
and any three yellow beasts. Note that buying the Fenumal Hybrid Arachnid beast from other players and adding it to your bestiary WILL NOT unlock the portal recipe and grant access to Fenumus' Lair. Player need to unlock the recipe prior. However, beastcrafting is account-bound which meant it can be done in previous league.
Abilities
Poison on hit
: The area has a
poison
on
hit
mod and triple poison
duration
.
Tail Spike
: Its basic
attacks
are ranged, throwing a somewhat slow dart with a low
attack speed
.
Tail Spike Swing
: It sometimes also throws a volley of darts instead.
Spit Mortar
: Throws a green
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
-like
projectile
that
forks
every time it hits the ground,
chaining
5 times.
Ground Stab
: Stabs the ground, spreading green webs which slow by 50% and deal nearly 2000
chaos
dps for 12s. A web also always appears under the character; this one does not spread.
Hallucinate
: Makes the edge of the screen blurry, other effects unknown, lasts 5s.
Summon Spiders
: Often summons at least 6 scorpion/spider minions.
Chaos Spark Spiders
: During the 2nd phase, every 12 seconds, 4 webbed stones will pulsate green before unleashing a swarm of little green spiders that deal
chaos damage
.
Strategy
Chaos resistance
goes a long way against Fenumus.
Fenumus has a low
attack speed
and only
attacks
from range, meaning if you continuously move around, its dart attack will miss you.
Spit Mortar and Ground Stab both have a wind-up, so you can be ready to dodge them. When you see it preparing ground stab, move away from it and continue moving to dodge the web appearing at your feet. For Spit Mortar, it'll shoot in the direction it's facing, so try to get behind it.  The
projectile
's first few forks won't be too large, but the later ones will practically engulf half the room.
At 50%
life
, you will be webbed and teleported to an underground mine. Any
ground effects
or
totems
will disappear, as well as
debuffs
on Fenumus.
The webbed stones will unleash their
chaos
spark when the 3rd green pulse ends, allowing you to easily position yourself to avoid getting shotgunned; single sparks do not deal much damages.
Do note that
poisons
will last longer than usual, which will notably delay
energy shield
recharge.
Exclusive drops
Fenumus will always drop one of the following
unique items
:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
The
beast
will also be captured by
Einhar
, unlocking the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "skill._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
craft
(add
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods.name"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
suffix to a non-
unique item
). The captured boss is an ingredient for this recipe.
Note that fighting this beast boss again at the
Blood Altar
in the
Menagerie
will not drop another
unique item
.
Version history
Version
Changes
3.2.0
Introduced to the game.

Fidelitas, the Mourning

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Fidelitas, the Mourning
is a unique monster located at the end of
The Chamber of Sins Level 2 (Act 2)
. He guards the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Attributes
Resists Elemental Damage
Resists Chaos
Background
Fidelitas was once a slave named Raulo.
He fell in love with
Maligaro
, who later demonstrated his new skill at imbuing humans with the calibric extantia on him. It is not clear if he liked this gift,
but he remained loyal to him. To honour this fidelity, the monster he had become was named Fidelitas.
Map variants
Stronger versions of Fidelitas serve as map bosses:
Loathe
- was
Flooded Mine Map
boss until
version 2.2.0
and
Overgrown Ruin Map
boss until
version 2.4.0
.

Garukhan, Queen of the Winds
is a minor
pantheon
goddess and optional boss in
Act 9
found in a side area of
The Quarry
.
She is the mother of
Shakari, Queen of the Sands
, and wife of
Sin
.
Quest
Garukhan must be defeated to complete
The Ruler of Highgate
, which awards the player with 1 passive skill point.
Strategy
As Garukhan is damaged she will summon twisters and storms to the arena. At 2/3rds and 1/3rd life she will disappear and summon Sand Goliaths, Sand Warriors and
Kira, Servant of Garukhan
.
Kira's spear attack applies
bleed
.
Pantheon power
+5% chance to Evade Attacks if you've taken a Savage Hit recently

divination cards
can drop from this monster:

located in
The Refinery
in
Act 9
.
Quests
Recurring Nightmare
Lore
Adus was mentioned in the book 1 of
The Purity Chronicles
as "Commander Adus of Highgate", which was one of the warriors under The High Templar
Voll
in the Purity Rebellion against Emperor
Chitus
and his Eternal Empire.
General Adus is also mentioned in
Lady Dialla
's (which was one of the mistresses of Chitus, and later given to
Malachai
) quest dialogue
The Eternal Nightmare
,
which she said: "...My Malachai did the crafting. Beautiful work. General Adus did the murdering..."
Finally, General Adus was mentioned as an eternal commander of the Highgate Legion. He was the slavemaster of
Karui
,
Maraketh
and
Ezomyte
slaves, forced the slaves to dig gems.
It is not sure the actual chronology of the historical events.

General Gravicius
General Gravicius

General Gravicius
is a boss in
Act 3
, located in
The Ebony Barracks
. Defeating him is the objective of the side quest
Sever the Right Hand
.
Strategy
For this boss,
fire resistance
is crucial. When at range he will spam his
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
skill and his
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.  His Molten shell does a lot of fire damage which could even 1 shot weaker characters. In addition to the fire damage he deals, 2 of his guards cast a curse (
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
) that reduces your fire resistance.
General Gravicius has a high
energy shield
, so it may take some time to defeat him. Do not be afraid to use a portal scroll to refill your potions if they run out.
Dialogue
Gravicius is voiced by New Zealand actor,
Daryl Habraken
.
Gravicius says a set dialogue aimed at the player when you approach him. This varies by class.
Duelist :
Give up the fight duelist,  Your soul was lost long ago!
Templar :
You're god's greatest disappointment.
Shadow:
You're not the only one who wields death as a weapon, boy!
Ranger/Scion :
God gave this world to Man, not to you, little girl!
Marauder :
You've forgotten your place, Karui slave!
Witch :
I shall purify you in flame, whore of nightmare!
Effects
Gravicius's firestorm effect has the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
which is a purchasable microtransaction for players to obtain.
Map variants
Stronger versions of General Gravicius can be found on the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
and the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
. The Colonnade Map version of the boss can be selected as one of the four random mini-bosses of the Shaper fight in the
The Shaper's Realm
.

General Marceus Lioneye
is a
boss
. He is the general of the Eternal Empire
Legion
.
He can be appeared in the game as a boss when using
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
and at least one other
Emblems
to open portals to the
Domain of Timeless Conflict
.
He can also appears as a boss in normal
Legion
encounter in map. However, the spawn chance is low.
Lore
Marceus Lioneye was a general of the Eternal Empire, and the leader of the Gemling Legion. A proud but skilled archer, he was best recognized for the virtue gem embedded in one of his eyes. During the Purity Rebellion, Lioneye was tasked with driving away
Kaom
's invading forces. His forces were at a great advantage, as the Karui warrior tradition forbade men from fighting with projectile weapons. However, Kaom realized that the tradition didn't apply to women, and so ordered his niece
Hyrri
to ambush Lioneye's forces with her band of female Karui archers as they left their fortifications to rout Kaom. Following his last stand and defeat at
Lioneye's Watch
, Kaom honored Lioneye's efforts by wearing his severed head on his belt.

PAGE: Gorgol Terror

Gorgol Terror
Gorgol Terror
Location(s)
Azurite Mine
Resistance(s)
Resists elemental damage
Resists chaos
This article is a
stub
. Please help
improve the article
by expanding it.
Gorgol Terror
is a unique boss located in the
Azurite Mine
. He may appear in
Azurite Fissure
(level 3 Azurite node).
In-Game
His fight takes place after clearing the usual Azurite encounter, and occurs in three phases. In the first, he will engage in normal combat. At half health, he will take a brief intermission to strafe the area with azurite fire. Shortly thereafter, he will return to the ground to attack the delver(s) again.
Version history
Version
Changes
3.4.0
Introduced to the game

Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
In 3.6.0, Jun is introduced in Highgate in Act 9.
Thus, Guff now can be encountered from Act 9 onwards only.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Great Webmother
CATEGORIES: Talisman bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Great_Webmother
======================================================================

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "COUNT(DISTINCT events._pageName)"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Talismans are infused with the power of the First Ones, the primeval gods of the ancient Ezomytes. The creatures of Wraeclast are instinctively drawn to these relics and the feral vigour they impart.
Description on the official Path of Exile website
The
Talisman leagues
were a pair of
challenge leagues
, one standard and the other hardcore. In these leagues, monsters are possessed by ancient Ezomyte
Talismans
. These monsters are indicated by green Talisman symbols over their heads. The Talismans grant them additional strength and properties in battle. Killing one of these monsters allows the player to claim its Talisman for himself.
The standard and hardcore versions have the same core mechanics but slightly different items available.
The leagues started on December 11, 2015 along with the release of
Version 2.1.0
and ended March 4, 2016.
Characters that died in standard Talisman league respawned in the standard Talisman league, while those that died in hardcore Talisman league respawned in
Standard league
. At the end of the Talisman leagues, all characters and
stashes
were moved to Standard league and
Hardcore league
.
Modifier
All areas had:
Ancient Talismans possess monsters throughout Wraeclast
Items
Talismans
Main page:
Talisman
Talismans are a type of
amulet
. All talismans are
corrupted
when dropped, so they cannot be modified. They have special implicit modifiers more powerful than ordinary amulets and can be normal, magic, rare, or unique. For the full list of talismans possession effects and other related information, see
Talisman
.
Shared between HC and SC
These base items were exclusive to the standard and hardcore Talisman leagues:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..

Talisman
article for details.

Charged Attack
Deals 60% of Damage
Flicker Strike
Deals 120% of Damage.
40% increased Attack Speed
Strategy
The fight will alternate between the following attacks and phases:
Melee Attacks:
Regular melee attacks, a Flicker Strike ability and an AoE Attack in a Cone directly in front of the Guardian.
Combo Attack:
A Red Circle appears around the Guardian, and a flurry of hits are dealt to anything in this zone.
Minions (Phase 2):
The Guardian disappears and Monsters enter the arena, they are much stronger than the average map ones: The phase ends after you beat a Unique Monster. This phase will occur three times throughout the fight as the Guardian is reduced to 75%, 50% and 25% of his HP.
Smoke (Phase 3):
The Most Dangerous phase. The Guardian disappears and patches of smoke appear in the Arena. While invisible, you will be repeatedly hit by attacks, with no ways to evade them through physically dodging. The only way to make this phase stop is to find the smoke cloud in which the Guardian is hiding. Once you've found the right smoke cloud, he will reappear, ceasing his attacks. This phase only happens thrice per fight, after each Minion phase.
Items found from this monster
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.

Guardian of the Minotaur
Guardian of the Hydra

Introduced to the game.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.7.4c
Fixed an instance crash that could occur during the
Guardian of the Minotaur
encounter.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects, Betrayal targets, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
3.2.0
No longer immune to Taunt, though they may not retarget based on being taunted.
3.0.0
Many unique monsters which were previously immune to Ailments have had their immunities removed, with the exception of Freeze. Notably, this includes several Beyond Demons, the Atziri Trio, Atziri herself, The Shaper and his Guardians, among others.
2.4.0
Introduced to the game.

Guardians of the Void

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, which is a
map fragment

Abilities
The following abilities are used by the
Guardian of the Phoenix
:
Default Attack
Deals 100% of Damage.
Whirling Charge
Deals 80% of Damage.
80% more Attack Speed.
Phoenix Firebomb
Deals 8781 to 13172 Fire Damage
Charges for 4 seconds
-50% Fire Resistance on Hit
In addition
, the following things happen while fighting the Guardian:
The player gains a debuff that reduces the maximum fire resistance by 1% This debuff is permanent until the boss dies. It stacks for every 10% health the boss is missing.
A Phoenix is summoned that shoots fire projectiles at the player. The phoenix is revived a few seconds after being killed. The less health the boss has, the more phoenixes are summoned.
A fire spout opens that summons a tornado which travels slowly around the room and deals fire damage in its area of effect. Each tornado disappears after a few seconds of activity and is replaced by a new one. The less health the boss has, the more spouts open.

The
Guardians of the Void
are the four bosses of certain Tier 16
maps
, who each drop a different map fragment required to fight
the Shaper
. They are the guardians of the Shaper.
Guardians
Name
Guardian of the Phoenix
Guardian of the Hydra
Guardian of the Minotaur
Guardian of the Chimera
Image
Map
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Main Drop
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Mechanics
The Uncharted Realms
Atlas passive skill tree that enhances drops from
The Shaper
,
The Elder
and from their respective guardians.
Occasionally, a Shaper-influenced Tier 16 map can drop, which has one of the four Shaper Guardian specific map bases. The map can be sold by
Zana
or offered in-map as part of her
Atlas Mission
. Player can use an
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
to reroll the map between the 4 Shaper Guardians Maps. However, player cannot roll other T16 Map into Shaper Guardians Maps, or vice versa.
Upon slaying the boss, they will drop a Shaper
Map fragment
that named after the boss (i.e.
Guardian of the Minotaur
drop
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
) Place a complete set of Shaper map fragments in the map device in correct order, can open 6 portals to fight
the Shaper
.
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lore
They are the main antagonists of
Atlas of Worlds
storyline, they are the guardians of the Void, which all 4 are needed to defeat to fight
Shaper
.
In
War for the Atlas
storyline, their maps would be occupied by
Elder Guardians
instead if the players beat all four Guardians of the Void and spread Elder influence to their maps.
Since
Conquerors of the Atlas
expansion, they do not appear in the epilogue questline nor on the
Atlas
, and they are thus assumed to be defeated and slain like
The Elder
. They are still accessible from Zana and from the Map Device.
In
Echoes of the Atlas
expansion, the Guardians of the Void are somehow back to the lore as bosses in
the Uncharted Realms
. They can be duplicated by the
Maven
and fight them all at once in
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
See also
Elder Guardians
Version history
Version
Changes
3.9.1
Zana Atlas Objectives can now offer
Shaper Guardian
Maps as options as long as you are in a Tier15+ Map.
3.9.0d
Fixed a bug where completing Shaper Guardians Maps caused all Maps in the Atlas of Worlds to be completed.
3.9.0
The Atlas of Worlds has been reworked. You now start at the centre and will work your way outwards as you explore a new storyline.
The Shaper and Elder storyline is gone and has been replaced by a story that takes place in the aftermath of the old story.
Zana can now sell Shaper- and Elder-influenced maps once you have progressed your Atlas enough. These act like the old Atlas influence, creating new monsters, threats, and rewards.
The Elder, Shaper, and combined Elder and Shaper encounters can still be accessed. If you're lucky, Zana may offer a Elder or
Shaper guardian'
s map!
2.4.1
The Guardians of the Void can no longer be permanently taunted. They can only be taunted every so often.
2.4.0
Introduced to the game.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.12.0
Life regeneration modifiers are now less likely to roll on items. The values of life regeneration granted by these modifiers has been increased across all but the first tier, with the top tiers now granting twice as much life regeneration per second than before. Existing items with these modifiers can be updated to the new values with a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
(Not in patch note) The Guatelitzi's mod has changed to
Regenerate (32-40) Life per second
(doubled)
3.10.0
Guatelitzi's
jewellery modifier now grants 2% increased maximum life (from 3 to 5%). The Energy Shield version now grants 3% increased maximum Energy Shield (from 7 to 10%). Using a Divine Orb will bring existing items to these new values.
3.5.0
Introduced to the core game
3.3.0
Introduced to the game as league mini-boss

Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
In 3.6.0, Jun is introduced in Highgate in Act 9.
Thus, Guff now can be encountered from Act 9 onwards only.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: High Templar Avarius
CATEGORIES: Act 5 unique monsters, Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/High_Templar_Avarius
======================================================================

High Templar Avarius
High Templar Avarius
Level(s)
43
Location(s)
The Chamber of Innocence
Quest(s)
Death to Purity
"Avarius" redirects here. For his Act 10 counterpart, see
Avarius, Reassembled
.
"Innocence" redirects here. For the NPC, see
Innocence (NPC)
.
High Templar Avarius
is a
unique boss
located in
The Chamber of Innocence
in
Act 5
. Upon defeating him, he transformed into the second form:
Innocence, God-Emperor of Eternity
.
Lore
After
Dominus
died (or during Dominus left Oriath for the expedition in
Wraeclast
) , Avarius took his place into the
Oriath
as the High Templar
that commands the
Ebony Legion
,
Blackguards
and other subordinate of the
Order of the Templar
. For some reason Avarius secretly became a vessel of god
Innocence
.
According to
Utula
, it is Avarius to command to build
The Templar Courts
and
The Chamber of Innocence
. It is not sure the Court of Divine Temperance (mentioned in older version of the
Letters of Exile
, which "Court of Divine Temperance" is currently removed from that environmental lore in-game) and The Templar Courts is the same location or not. Based on
epilogue
quest
Picking Up the Pieces
, the previous High Templar Dominus has an office in
The Fallen Courts
(which is the same location as
The Templar Courts
of Act 5)
Quests
Death to Purity
Strategy
Avarius represents a significant paradigm shift in how boss fights occur in Path of Exile: Unlike most such enemies encountered in
Act 1
through
Act 4
, High Templar Avarius and his subsequent form, Innocence, are capable of inflicting massive amounts of
elemental damage
through specific attacks and spells. Withstanding said attacks is simply not viable for most characters coursing normally through the game, as even capped
resistances
will not ensure the player's survival when hit by the boss' most devastating abilities.
Instead, the player should focus on dodging these attacks and spells while dishing out as much damage as possible in order to shorten the fight.
Movement skills
can greatly help when trying to accomplish this while adequate
defences
, such as capped resistances combined with high eHP pool, should provide a slight safety net.
Some attacks and spells has clear graphics to indicate the player to dodge them, such as the delayed blast , the
Divine Beam
that chase you, etc. While the slow moving "bullet hell", the player can hide in any statue.
Abilities
Innocence, God-Emperor of Eternity
At every phase, Avarius will repeatedly cast basic abilities followed by a Major ability. Avarius will always utter a key phrase just before he uses any of his major abilities.
Initial phase
:
Basic abilities:
Cleave
: A circle appears around Avarius, then he strikes with increased range.
Fireball
: Avarius casts 3 fireballs.  The fireballs appear to converge on a single point.
Major abilities:
Divine Slam
: Avarius' staff glows, and he casts a wave on the floor that travels to a location.  Upon reaching the destination, the wave explodes in a large area.
Key Phrase:
"
Kneel!
"
Rapid Projectile:
Avarius draws a pentacle, channeling energy to release 5 projectiles in a barrage after a short delay. These projectiles always pierce.
Key Phrase:
"
Burn!
"
Raise Statue
: After losing 25% of his life, Avarius will randomly animate the large statues in the arena.  The statues attack with a melee sword swing.
Key Phrase:
'
"Rise!"'
Minion Spawn:
At 50% life, Avarius teleports to the middle of the arena and spawns minions for 24 seconds.  During this period, all Statues disappear, and Avarius is invincible.
Key Phrase:
'
"I am the Emperor!"'
Innocence phase:

: Avarius dashes to a location, leaving

projectiles in his wake. No Key phrase.

: Avarius dashes to a location and slams the ground in front of him shortly after arriving. No Key phrase.

: Avarius casts a linear ray at the player that inflicts fire damage over time and a stacking debuff that increases fire damage taken by 10% per stack, similar to
Scorching Ray
.

.

Innocence releases a projectile to the floor, after landing it will emit 5 waves of projectiles in a nova. The statues will block any projectiles in their trajectory.

"

======================================================================
PAGE: Hillock
CATEGORIES: Act 1 unique monsters, Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Hillock
======================================================================

For the
Immortal Syndicate
member, see
Hillock, the Blacksmith
.
Hillock
Hillock
Level(s)
1
Location(s)
The Twilight Strand (Act 1)
Quest(s)
Enemy at the Gate
Skill(s)
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Hillock
(known as
Hillock of Slaugh
in the
Letters of Exile
) is a unique monster in
The Twilight Strand
in
Act 1
.
Defeating him is the objective of the quest
Enemy at the Gate
.
Lore
Hillock, just like the player character and the
Dying Exile
, are exiled from
Oriath
. When player character has arrived
The Twilight Strand
, Hillock is a zombie already.
Also, according to the
Letters of Exile
, Hillock is exiled for :
Sentenced: 1599 IC
Transgressions:
Mortal Wrath
Six counts of Common Homicide
One count of Infanticide
Two counts of Homicide with post-mortem Desecration
Unlawful Avarice
Twelve counts of Extortion through Threat of Mortal Harm
Seven counts of Armed Larceny
Two counts of Armed Larceny resulting in Mortal Harm
Unsolicited Lust
Four counts of Rape
Two counts of Attempted Rape
Mortal Wrath
One count of Rape resulting in Mortality
The monster used to be harder in higher difficulties, which uses
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
However, the game became a single
difficulty
, 10 acts game, instead of 3 difficulties in 4 acts (the game was in 3 loops just like classic ARPG
Diablo 2
or
Titan Quest
) since version 3.0.0, so that the monster has only one variant during version 3.0.X.
But since version 3.1, the developer introduced a hidden version which use a supporter-designed sword
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
(visually, the monster apparently does not have the effect from the mod of the sword) and (since version 3.2.0) have a special Leap Slam that also summon zombie on where he lands.
This hidden version is known as
Uber Hillock
among the player community. There is no lore to explain his appearance however, nor why he would be spawned only after the player reset the area and grind the area again and again and apparently the spawn chance is tied to the character level.
"
What? I'm free? You did this. Well, anything is better than being stuck in that. His mindless thirst fell far short of My desires, simple though they are. But now there's you. So vital. So defiant. So take Me. This dying world is but kindling, yet its un-quickened blood may boil once again. And I've waited so long for a real bath.
"
- A voice from
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
upon killing Uber Hillock
The game developer later further revised the storyline so that the corpse of Hillock is resurrected as
Hillock, the Blacksmith
some time after the
Act 1
.
Before the release of the
Letters of Exile
in
version 0.10.8
, the game developer also released a lore in their official website,
which was later took down due to website redesign. That lore mentioned Hillock's occupation and how he was killed and how he was rediscovered as a zombie. However, that lore did not mention Hillock was exiled nor his crimes such as rape, so that it is not certain that it is still
canon
or not.
Strategy: Normal Hillock
Defeating Hillock is easy. Use your equipped skill gem for more damage(RMB or W), when out of mana refill (hotkey 5 by default initial setup). If he gets a few hits on you, refill your HP (hotkey 1 or 2). Regular attack when needed (LMB or Q).
At around 50% of his life Hillock pulls the sword out of his chest, which will increase his attack speed while also restoring a portion of his life.
Strategy: Uber Hillock
Uber Hillock. The sword on the chest is Oni-Goroshi instead of generic sword in the normal Hillock
There is a very low chance for Hillock to spawn as "Uber Hillock". The sword on his chest is replaced with an
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, which drops on death. He has more life and damage and can do a
Leap Slam
that summons zombies like his map boss counterparts.
Because the
The Twilight Strand
becomes inaccessible when the character enters Lioneye's Watch after killing Hillock, obtaining Oni-Goroshi from Hillock requires the following, somewhat "grindy" procedure:
With a new character, kill Hillock and optionally every other monster in the Twilight Strand.
Do not enter
Lioneye's Watch
but log out to the character selection screen, wait about 1 minute 30 seconds for the instance to reset (the Twilight Strand has a shorter reset period than other zones), and reenter the game with the same character. This puts the character back at the beginning of the Twilight Strand.
Continue killing the monsters and Hillock until he carries the sword. This can even last until your character is level 9.
To speed up this procedure, a batch of three or four new characters can be used in serial to farm Hillock in this manner (each Hillock kill takes about 30 seconds with +10% movement speed boots). Furthermore, using Scion will allow you to take movement speed nodes on the bottom right of the tree, hence allowing you to gain experience at a faster rate.
Video Tutorial:
Map variants
Stronger versions of Hillock serve as map bosses:

Version history
Version
Changes
3.15.0
We have rebalanced various monsters found throughout Act One to make them more dangerous. Note, the balance changes made to Act One Bosses and monsters also apply to their Endgame Map versions.
Monster life has been increased by roughly 46% at level 1, tapering down to the same amount of life at level 84. Some bosses and monsters in Act 1, such as Hillock and Oozeback have had their life reduced to partially counteract this change.
Various effects improvements have been made to monsters and bosses throughout Act One.
Various audio improvements have been made to monsters and bosses throughout Act One.
3.7.0
Improved the early-game experience. Fights in the first three Acts will be tougher but more responsive, involved and dynamic. This includes fights against basic monsters!
3.2.0
The
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
version of
Hillock
has more life, deals more damage, and has learned a new trick.
3.1.1c
(Not stated explicitly in patch note) The
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
version of
Hillock
has been added to the game
3.0.0
Cruel and Merciless version of Hillock were removed due to overhaul of the game.
1.3.0c
Fixed a bug where there could be a short clientside freeze when Hillock was killed.
1.1.3
Removed the invulnerability period from Hillock when he is drawing his sword in all difficulties.
1.0.6
Hillock
now uses the same triggered effect that
the Blacksmith
does, in Cruel and Merciless difficulties. The damage of the poison cloud produced by his zombies has been reduced in all instances (including Maps).
0.11.0
Hillock
now correctly restores a percentage of his maximum life when he draws his sword. This life restoration increases in higher difficulties.
Before
0.10.0
Introduced to the game

Hillock, the Blacksmith
is one of the 18
Immortal Syndicate
members.
Lore
Hillock
is the name of the first boss of the entire game, located in
The Twilight Strand (Act 1)
. It is suggested by the in-game dialogues that the
Mastermind
resurrected the corpse.
Before Hillock was killed by the player character in Act 1, he was a "mindless" zombie already, so apparently after the Mastermind resurrected Hillock, still affects his ability to think.
Before the
open beta
, the game developer released a lore in their website regarding Hillock, which mentioned he was a blacksmith.
Despite the lore is not certain to be still
canon
and the webpage is not accessible anymore, a map boss that is based on Hillock,
The Blacksmith
, is named after the occupation. Not all the names of the map boss have a logical explanation.
This resurrected boss, now known as "Hillock, the Blacksmith", now has safehouse rewards that matching the nickname. As a member of Immortal Syndicate, he rewards the player in the
Syndicate Safehouse
by adding
quality
to items (weapon, body armour and flask; map until 3.14). Note that
Abyss Scarab
is added in version 3.14.0 and apparently arbitrarily linked to Hillock.

"
Want a friend, I do. Sit in my room, bashing my hammer, talking to myself... gettin' ANGRY... with a friend maybe I wouldn't hurt people no more. Will you help me find a friend?
"
- Hillock
"
My head still hurts!
"
- Hillock
"
Maybe this will make the bad thoughts go...
"
- Hillock (after the player let Hillock escape)

Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
In 3.6.0, Jun is introduced in Highgate in Act 9.
Thus, Tora now can be encountered from Act 9 onwards only.
Several veiled modifiers now have fewer tiers. Low tiers contribute more towards unlocking higher tiers, however it will, on average, take more unveils to unlock the highest tier.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.

.
Invasion Bosses
are unique monsters, introduced in
Invasion league
. After the league ended they were not implemented into the core game, but are still accessible in:
Trespassers
Atlas passive skill
of
Tirn's End
atlas region
Maps
with the
Invasion league
mod crafted on them (through
Zana's map device
).

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods.name"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
signature modifier
.
Tempest
areas with the
of Incursion
suffix. (Tempest itself only appears with Tempest league map mod or other sources that spawn Tempest; however in core gameplay Tempest are only spawn with prefix only)
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
prophecy
Short-term
race
leagues with the
Invasion
mod. (GGG did not carry race any more)
Flashback
events
(Not available in core game)
Leaguestone
Note that invasion bosses have more difficult skills and modifiers at higher
monster levels
. This page attempts to list all of the bosses' possible attributes.
In an area where an Invasion Boss can spawn, it can spawn anywhere in the area, usually accompanied by support monsters of normal rarity.

Lore

Breach monsters
known as
It That Reaches
,

It That Whispers
,
It of Tul
, etc.
It That Fled also has Breach-related reward in Safehouse.

Legacy mods
Since
version 3.6.0
, It That Fled's signature mods have been re-tiered, from 4 tiers to 3. These generation 1 legacy crafted mods can only be found in items in permanent leagues:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
The lowest tier of the craftable mod that introduced in 3.6.0, is retired since
version 3.14.0
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
In 3.6.0, Jun is introduced in Highgate in Act 9.
Thus, Tora now can be encountered from Act 9 onwards only.
It That Fled's veiled mod (non-Chaos added as extra Chaos) has been split into Physical added as Chaos, Lightning added as Chaos, Cold added as Chaos, and Fire added as Chaos, and the values have been adjusted. New powerful versions of these modifiers can now appear in the basic mod pool on weapons and shields at higher levels.
Several veiled modifiers now have fewer tiers. Low tiers contribute more towards unlocking higher tiers, however it will, on average, take more unveils to unlock the highest tier.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.
References

======================================================================
PAGE: Izaro
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters, Labyrinth unique monsters

======================================================================

Izaro
Izaro
Level(s)
33 / 55 / 68 / 75
Location(s)
Aspirant's Trial
(
The Lord's Labyrinth
)
Health
112 404 (Normal)
571 634 (Cruel)
3 296 085 (Merciless)
8 813 933 (Eternal)
Damage
385 (Normal)
1315 (Cruel)
2575 (Merciless)
3904 (Eternal)
ApS
2.25
Resistance(s)
15/25/45/45%
Fire
15/25/45/45%
Cold
15/25/45/45%
Lightning
7/13/22/22%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
Cannot Be Knocked Back
33% Less Curse Effectiveness
Izaro
is the boss of
The Lord's Labyrinth
. He is encountered three times throughout the labyrinth, in the Aspirant's Trial room.
Lore
Izaro Phrecius was the last emperor of the royal Phrecius family, and the third-to-last Emperor of the Eternal Empire. Unable to produce an heir and desperate to find a worthy successor, an obscure text detailing the ancient Azmerian traditions of Ascendancy inspired Izaro to commission the construction of
the Labyrinth
, using it as his inspiration. He declared that anyone who can pass his Labyrinth will be declared the next emperor.
Chitus Perandus
was the first person to do so.
When the aging Izaro retired and gave the throne to Chitus on the First Kaso of Verusi, 1319 IC, the new emperor immediately cast Izaro into the Lord's Labyrinth and sealed the gates behind him. Twinned with the Goddess he worshipped, Emperor Izaro lives on in the Labyrinth.
Abilities
Skill
Effect
Damage
N.
C.
M.
Ground Slam
Raises his weapon up then slams the ground, dealing heavy physical damage in an area in front of him. Deals more damage the closer you are to the center of the AoE. Only used while Izaro is wielding a two-handed mace.
Radiant Slash
Winds up and slashes, releasing a huge expanding projectile forward, dealing heavy damage. Only used while Izaro is wielding dual swords.
Leap Slam
Leaps at the player and slams the ground, dealing heavy physical damage.
Shield Bash
Charges up, then dashes forward and swings his shield, dealing heavy physical damage on contact. Almost identical's to Voll's attack.
Sunder
Sends a wave that travels forward. On hit, creates a shockwave that deals damage in an area. The wave can hit the same target multiple times.
Spikes
Izaro strikes the ground and channels, releasing spikes from the ground at the player's location. The spiky ground deals damage over time and slows.
Izaro is assisted by
the Goddess of Justice
, which attacks independently from Izaro.
Skill
Effect
Damage
N.
C.
M.
Mortar Barrage
Lobs mortars towards the player, dealing Fire damage in an area when they land.
Summon Guardians
Bombards a random area, each explosion summoning a skeleton.
Teleport
Casts a green circle around Izaro and a random area in a trapped area. After a delay, anyone within Izaro's circle will be teleported to the other circle. Only used on the 3rd fight, on Cruel and Merciless.
Strategy
Izaro Phrecius
Izaro's health thresholds
First Two Encounters
The first two encounters with Izaro add new mechanics to the fight that carry on to the next encounter(s). The mechanics change daily alongside the layout of The Labyrinth. Izaro will be encountered in a room containing three small holes in the ground that will introduce the additional mechanics for that particular fight. A Forbidden Reliquary room contains a
Labyrinth Trinket
which makes the mechanics easier to deal with.
Any mechanics that have not been dealt with will carry over for the subsequent fights. However, leaving the mechanics untouched (Ex. don't destroy any portals, have all three idols activate, don't activate any charge disruptors, etc.) will result in Izaro dropping an additional
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
on death.
Conduits
"When one defiles the effigy, one defiles the emperor."
Three statues, one for each element, are located in each corner of the room. While active, the statues provide Izaro with increased damage, added damage, and resistance to the corresponding element. The statues can be clicked to deactivate them for a short time.
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, they will disable the first conduit they click permanently. Any statues active when Izaro is defeated continue to provide their benefits through any remaining encounters. If the player brings Izaro to the next phase while all conduits are active, the player is qualified for an additional Treasure Key drop. Conduits still underground count as active when the phase ends.
Charge Disruptors
"Complex machinations converge to a single act of power."
Izaro will gain charges throughout the encounter, granting him bonuses to damage, speed, and defense. Izaro can have a maximum of 10 charges on him. Charges can be removed by activating one of three Charge Disruptors. Each Charge Disruptor can only be used once. When Izaro has 10 charges he will say
"
Behold the machinery at maximum efficiency!
"
.
If the player possesses
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first Charge Disruptor you use will reactivate shortly afterwards, allowing one additional use. Any charges active when Izaro is defeated will persist permanently for any remaining encounters. Not activating any Charge Disruptors
and
leaving Izaro at maximum charges at the end of the fight will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop.
Elemental Essences
"The essence of an empire must be shared equally amongst all of its citizens."
Three elemental essences,
Burning Essence
,
Freezing Essence
and
Crackling Essence
, that take the form of Golems will join the fight one after another. After rising out of the floor, they will slowly walk towards Izaro, attempting to bond with him. These golems can be killed during this period. If they touch Izaro, they will disappear and confer their bonuses to him for the remainder of the fight, which persists for future encounters. Each golem grants additional damage, resistance, and chance of applying a status ailment of their respective element.
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first essence the player damages will be instantly killed. Any essence that is not killed will confer its bonuses to Izaro for the remaining fights and returns as an enemy, but will attack the player instead of attempting to bond with Izaro. Having all three essences bond with Izaro or remain alive when the trial ends will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop.
Gargoyles
"Some things that slumber should never be awoken."'
Izaro will summon three gargoyles to join him in the fight. Each gargoyle initially starts inactive and has an extremely high amount of damage reduction, making it very difficult to kill. When a gargoyle activates it will lose its damage reduction and channel a beam, indicating which gargoyle has activated. Each gargoyle grants different bonuses to Izaro:
Granite-infused Gargoyle grants damage reduction
Basalt-infused Gargoyle grants increased damage dealt
Quicksilver-infused Gargoyle grants increased movement speed (and attack speed?)
Steel-imbued Gargoyle (unknown)
After the gargoyle finishes channeling, it will become mobile and attack the player. Once a gargoyle is killed, Izaro will lose his bonuses from the respective gargoyle.
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first gargoyle the player damages will be instantly killed. Any gargoyles that are left alive will permanently confer its bonuses to Izaro and returns in remaining fights as enemies. Leaving all three gargoyles alive will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop.
Idols
"Slowness lends strength to one's enemies."
Three Idols will spawn in each corner, one for each element. These Idols start with minimum Life and cannot die. Each Idol will slowly regenerate Life over time. Once they reach full Life, the idol will cast Fire/Ice/Lightning Beacon, creating multiple delayed explosion in random areas that deals elemental damage (the same explosions as Flame/Frost/Storm Bearers Bloodlines enemies), then return to minimum Life.
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first Idol a player attacks will not regenerate life. If an idol has activated at least once, it will return in the remaining fights in an unreachable area. Having all three idols activate at least once will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop.
Lieutenants
"An emperor is only as efficient as those he commands."
Three skeletal Lieutenants will spawn in each corner, one at a time. Each Lieutenant corresponds to an element and have different abilities. The three Lieutenants are
Lieutenant of the Mace
(Fire),
Lieutenant of Rage
(Cold),
Lieutenant of the Bow
(Lightning).
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first Lieutenant the player damages will be instantly killed. Any Lieutenants left alive will return in the remaining fights. Leaving all three Lieutenants alive will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop. Lieutenants still underground count as alive when the phase ends.
Summoning Portals
"The Emperor beckons, and the world attends."
Three portals will spawn in each corner, one at a time. Each portal continuously summons Skeletal Mages of a specific element. Once the portal is destroyed, it will stop spawning skeletons.
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first portal the player damages will be instantly destroyed. Any portal left intact will return in the remaining fights. Leaving all three portals intact will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop. Portals still underground count as alive when the phase ends.
Fonts
"It is the sovereign who empowers the sceptre. Not the other way round."
Three fonts will spawn in each corner, one at a time. Each font casts a curse on the player.
Font of Fragility
casts
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
,
Font of Lethargy
casts
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, and
Font of Elements
casts
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
. Fonts can be damaged, and when destroyed they cast an empowered version of its curse on the player for 10 seconds. The curses cast by the fonts are affected by curse removal and immunity effects.
If the player possesses a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, the first font the player damages will be instantly destroyed. Any fonts left intact will return in the remaining fights. Leaving all three fonts intact will qualify for an additional Treasure Key drop. Fonts still underground count as alive when the phase ends.
Third Encounter
Izaro's teleport ability
The third and final mechanic will add two different Labyrinth traps (plus sentinel traps in Endgame Labyrinth) to the room the encounter takes place in (not during normal). On Cruel and higher difficulties, Izaro's Goddess of Justice can use Teleport, casting a green circle around Izaro, which teleports the player after a delay into a trap-filled area.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.3.0
Fixed a bug where Izaro could introduce Argus after the player had already snuck in and killed Izaro's most loyal friend.
3.2.0
Fixed a bug where Izaro's curse pylons would say they cast an empowered curse on death in the Cruel Labyrinth when they would not.
Fixed a bug where Izaro's curse pylons would still use the old Vulnerability curse when casting their Empowered Vulnerability.
3.0.1b
Fixed a bug with Izaro mechanics which could lead to an incompletable Labyrinth.
3.0.0
Killing Izaro now immediately disables the traps in his room.
Removed the traps from Izaro's final room during the Normal Labyrinth.
2.6.0
Removed the invulnerability buff applied to Izaro when he is waiting to flee from his arena. This fixes an issue where Izaro would attack, but couldn't be attacked back, preventing Life Leech.
2.4.0
Fixed a bug where Izaro couldn't be cursed by the same types of curses that his pylons could apply to him as an aura.
2.3.0
Cruel
Izaro has had his life reduced by 20% and the damage of his various skills reduced: His
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
and
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
damage values have been reduced by 12.5%. His
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
,
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
and Goddess Spirit Mortar Barrage damage have been reduced by 10%.
2.2.2
Fixed an issue where Izaro would not leave until the last Conduit had risen.
2.2.1d
Fixed a bug where the third encounter of Izaro would sometimes not have Izaro.
2.2.1
Izaro has been adjusted to be easier in Normal Difficulty. His life has been reduced by 20%. Izaro's Ground Slam and Radiant Slash now deal 10% less damage. The life of Gargoyles, Idols, Portals (Thresholds), Essences, Fonts and Lieutenants has been reduced by 20%. The Goddess of Justice's Mortar Barrage has been reduced by 20% damage, and she no longer uses Teleport. These changes only apply to Normal difficulty.
2.2.0
Introduced to the game.

Lore
"
You have heard of the famed Perandus family, I have no doubt. Wealthy and powerful and responsible for putting Malachai in position to bring Wraeclast to ruin all those centuries ago. Though Emperor Chitus is the most famous of the Perandus family, some remnants of his vile bloodline linger even still.
Janus was one such remnant. He too was orphaned, but with the Perandus coffers dry and the Perandus name worth its weight in gold, no one took him in. No one except my akhara.
That he is the only other survivor, and is now a powerful member of the Immortal Syndicate leaves no doubt in my mind that it was he who sold us out.
If there is but one silver lining in all of this, it is that I may get to sink my blades into his flesh over, and over, and over again.
"
-
Jun
"Janus Perandus" dialogue
"
At last, a chance to drive a sword through that Perandus skull.
"
-

I'm going to kill you Janus... Just not yet.
"
-
Jun
(after the player allow Janus to escape)
Jun speculated that Janus is the traitor that sold the Order of the Djinn to the Immortal Syndicate, which the Syndicate murdered every single members of the order except Jun.

======================================================================
PAGE: King Kaom
CATEGORIES: Act 4 unique monsters, Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/King_Kaom
======================================================================

King Kaom
King Kaom
Location(s)
Kaom's Stronghold
Quest(s)
The King of Fury
Resistance(s)
Resists
Elemental Damage
Resists
Chaos Damage
King Kaom
is a unique boss in
Kaom's Stronghold
. Defeating him is required for the quest
The King of Fury
.
Lore
King Kaom of the Karui concept art. It also used as the art for a Steam trading card.
Kaom
was the king of the Karui, often cited for his bravery and leadership. In the Karui's home,
Ngamakanui
, Kaom received a sign
(
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
) saying the Purity Rebellion needed his support against Emperor Chitus. The Karui were called by
High Templar Voll
to overthrow
General Marceus Lioneye
in the outer empire. Kaom accepted and led his people to
The Coast
in Wraeclast using canoes. The battle against Lioneye and his army of Gemlings has been written in
The Purity Chronicles
Book 3:
Fall of a Jade Axe
: a batallion of female archers caught Lioneye by suprise. Among these archers was
Hyrri
, who has studied archery with Voll's finest military tutors. She was of great importance in this battle.
When Lioneye fell, Kaom infamously wore his head on his belt.
In-game
After he had conquered
Lioneye's Watch
the Karui passed through the land with their Canoes. Their stories have been preserved on
weathered carvings
throughout Act 1. These carvings tell of the nightmares they encounter, the undead. Kaom has tried to fight it, praying to his ancestors and eventually received a vision he thought divine, but it was actually from Nightmare.
He took a group of warriors and entered the
Highgate
mines to join the
Beast
.
After the
The Awakening
however, more carvings in
Kaom's Stronghold
reveal the black spirit may have not only lead him and his warriors farther into Wraeclast, he had been corrupted by
Malachai
to slaughter his own men as a 'sacrifice'.
After Kaom left, Hyrri led the Karui back to
Ngamakanui
.
Related items
Kaom's trademark was his two handed axe and his belt/body armour, which wore the head of his nemesis Lioneye.
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Dialogue
King Kaom is voiced by New Zealand actor,
Andre King
.
References

Kitava's Heart
Kitava's Heart
Location(s)

,
The Feeding Trough
Quest(s)
The Ravenous God
An End to Hunger
Kitava's Heart
is a part of Kitava that spawns during the battle with
Kitava, the Insatiable
, which occurs in Act 5 and Act 10.
Version history
Version
Changes
3.2.0
Kitava's Heart can no longer be slowed below their base speed.
3.0.0
Introduced to the game as boss monster.

Kitava, The Destroyer
Kitava, The Destroyer
Location(s)
Lava Lake Map
Kitava, The Destroyer
is the map boss of the
Lava Lake Map
, and is similar to
Kitava, the Insatiable
in
Act 10
. It can be used to capture the
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Mechanics
Players must damage Kitava down with the help of
Sin
and
Innocence
The boss will disappear behind the Arena at 50% and 25% health, and
The Lord's Heart
will appear.
Players must kill the heart and then the boss will reappear and the battle continues.
When the boss reaches 0% health he will disappear and the battle ends
Abilities
X Blast
Deals X to Y Physical Damage
70% less damage to Minions
Swipe
Deals 10% of Damage
70% less damage to Minions
Beam Sweep
Deals X to Y Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
30% less damage to Minions
X Nuke
Deals X to Y Physical Damage
90% less damage to Minions
Death Breath
Deals X to Y Fire Damage
50% less damage to Minions
Devouring Darkness
Deals X Physical Damage per second
Base duration is 12 seconds
Base secondary duration is 4 seconds
70% less damage to Minions
Beam Orb 1
Deals X to Y Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Base duration is 15 seconds
60% less damage to Minions
Ultimate Death Breath
Deals X to Y Fire Damage
95% less damage to Minions
Beam Orb 2
Deals X to Y Physical Damage
50% of Physical Damage Converted to Fire Damage
Base duration is 20 seconds
60% less damage to Minions

Kitava's first form in Act 5
Kitava, the Insatiable
is the final boss of
Act 5
and
Act 10
. He is the main antagonist of
The Fall of Oriath
expansion. The battle consists of multiple phases in which players must damage Kitava, then damage
Kitava's Heart
, and this occurs alternatively till Kitava is defeated.
Lore
Kitava is the
Karui
god of corruption. According to Karui tradition, Kitava is a cannibal deity with boundless hunger. He has the power to consume and corrupt the souls of people, like he did with
King Kaom
. Like the other gods of Wraeclast, Kitava's abilities are restricted by
the Beast
(which was destroyed by the player in
Act 4
).
There are several artifacts related to Kitava:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Quest
The Ravenous God
An End to Hunger
Strategy
Kitava deals mostly
physical damage
and
fire damage
. High
armour
will help mitigate the
physical damage
, while supporting
flasks
will help reduce received damage.
Recommended flasks are
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
,
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
, and/or
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
. If you're using an evasion based defence,
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Life Regeneration
will help against Devouring Darkness, which can be assisted with various tools such as a
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Taking
Soul of Abberath
from the
Pantheon
can also help with Kitava's Ultimate Death Breath skill.
Kitava permanently lowers all of your
resistances
by 30% after "
defeating
" him at the end of
Act 5
(you can't win. You can only get him to about 50% health and then he turns on Enrage Mode, turns red, and fires a beam from his eyes that destroys the
Purity of Innocence
. Sin saves you at the last minute) , and reduces them by a further 30% after being
defeated
in
act 10
. Caution should be taken to make sure your resistances are high enough so the drop doesn't affect you too harshly. The effect is permanent and cannot be removed.
Kitava has side bosses in heart phases, primarily
Kitava's Heart
and one additional side boss being
Champion of the Feast
and
Shaman of the Feast
.

Kole
is a unique monster located in
The Lunaris Temple Level 1 (Act 3)
. He is similar to
Brutus, Lord Incarcerator
from
The Upper Prison
. He has a high-damage Ground Smash attack and he can pull the player towards him with his chain.
Lore
According to Grigor, Kole was an Oriathan rapist before Piety used him in her experiments.
Map Variants

Carnage
, was found in the

from game
version 2.4.0
until
version 3.1.0
.

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "mods.name"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..

, a member of Redblade.

Version history
Version
Changes
3.14.0
Modifiers that are unveiled through Jun are now more powerful than before. Previously, when an item was unveiled, the veiled modifier added to the item would be equal to the highest tier modifier available on the Crafting Bench. Now, the modifier being unveiled no longer counts as a crafted modifier and will be stronger than any craftable veiled modifier.
3.9.0
Monster resistances throughout the game have been reviewed and made more consistent. In many cases this has resulted in more resistances on monsters.
3.6.0
Bosses which previously had 60% or 80% less effect of curses on them now have 33% or 66% less effect of curses on them respectively. This includes map bosses, Incursion architects,
Betrayal targets
, Breachlords, Shaper, Elder and others.
In 3.6.0, Jun is introduced in Highgate in Act 9.
Thus, Tora now can be encountered from Act 9 onwards only.
Several veiled modifiers now have fewer tiers. Low tiers contribute more towards unlocking higher tiers, however it will, on average, take more unveils to unlock the highest tier.
3.5.0
Introduced to the game.

Trivia
A parody superstition says that Kuduku is the god of random number generation (RNGod) and offering him items can increase the chance of favourable results when using orbs, especially
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
.
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
prophecy pays homage to this joke.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kurgal, the Blackblooded
CATEGORIES: Boss monsters, Bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Kurgal,_the_Blackblooded
======================================================================

Kurgal, the Blackblooded
Kurgal, the Blackblooded
Kurgal, the Blackblooded
Location(s)
Abyssal Sanctum
(also known as The Lich's Tomb)
Resistance(s)
30%
Fire
30%
Cold
30%
Lightning
15%
Chaos
Modifier(s)
66% Less Hex Curse Effectiveness
Cannot Be Fully Slowed
Immune To Knockback
Unwavering
Kurgal, the Blackblooded
is a boss that can only appear inside
The Lich's Tomb
nodes within the
Abyssal City
biome, inside
The Azurite Mine
in
Delve
, at a depth of 110 or greater.
The encounter with Kurgal, the Blackblooded has two distinct phases, with an add phase during the initial phase.
The boss room is known as
Abyssal Sanctum
. From the boss room, the exit portal leads to "Abyssal Span".
Arena
The arena for the fight is called Abyssal Sanctum. The arena is comprised of two circles, with four low walls around the first circle and wide passages between them at 45 degree angles.
Skills
Kurgal, the Blackblooded has several different
skills
, focused primarily on physical damage, with some skills having 15-20% chaos conversion and 80% lightning conversion.
Basic Attack - Shoots arrows and wand projectiles.
Reflecting Beam - Casts a beam that deals 2841 physical damage per second to an area on the ground near the player, the area follows the player as they move around the arena and this lasts for up to 15 seconds. Players have between 0.2 and 0.45 seconds to avoid being hit by the initial spell.
Firestorm - Occasionally casts a medium sized firestorm that will land in the outer part of the arena, dealing physical and chaos damage over 6 seconds, every 0.2 seconds.
Chest Pulse - Casts a single laser pulse dealing physical damage, facing the direction of the player at the time of casting with a distance equal to the radius of the arena.
Ground Laser - 20 ground lasers are distributed evenly at two radii in the arena, in the first phase they occasionally begin connecting diagonally in the outer region of the arena with damaging beams dealing physical damage. In the second phase they occasionally spin slowly in groups dealing physical and chaos damage.
Transition Beam - Kurgal spreads his arms 180 degrees apart and launches two beams that close inwards towards his direction of facing leaving 20 degrees whereupon the spell finishes. This deals high physical damage with 15% converted to chaos.
The Fight
Approaching Kurgal initiates the fight.
During the first phase Kurgal will slowly and continually summon pillars surrounded by three glowing green circles. The pillars can appear in one of four preset locations nearest the player at the time and near the passages between the inner and outer circles. These pillars block player movement. It can detonate these pillars when between 1 and 4 are active given that the zone is not in darkness or in phase two. The area very near these glowing circles deals a large amount of damage to the player and any of these pillars that are nearby the player will explode simultaneously. Most often, all of the pillars will explode.
Occasionally it also places a small totem which erupts after a short duration to deal a small amount of damage, near the player.
Around the arena are 20 evenly-spaced totems, 8 are placed in the first radius and 12 are placed in the second radius - around the perimeter of the arena. During the first phase these connect in a triangular or diagonal pattern dealing minor physical damage to the player if they cross the beam.
When the boss reaches 50% of its initial health, it will become invulnerable, untargetable and begin to spawn abyssal montsers. These deal a small amount of damage and grant flask charges.
In the second half of the first phase, the fight resumes as before.
When the boss reaches low health, it begins its empowered phase.
During the second phase, the 20 totems begin to activate in groups spinning and dealing physical and chaos damage with a short laser beam.
It also launches projectiles frequently at the player which deal up to 8986 damage, 80% being lightning, and 20% being chaos.
It will also cast two spells at set intervals, either the Chest Pulse or Transition Beam, both of which are deadly but avoidable.
Items found from this monster
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..

Version

======================================================================
PAGE: Legion of Lost Souls
CATEGORIES: Talisman bosses
URL: https://pathofexile.fandom.com/wiki/Legion_of_Lost_Souls
======================================================================

Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "COUNT(DISTINCT events._pageName)"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Talismans are infused with the power of the First Ones, the primeval gods of the ancient Ezomytes. The creatures of Wraeclast are instinctively drawn to these relics and the feral vigour they impart.
Description on the official Path of Exile website
The
Talisman leagues
were a pair of
challenge leagues
, one standard and the other hardcore. In these leagues, monsters are possessed by ancient Ezomyte
Talismans
. These monsters are indicated by green Talisman symbols over their heads. The Talismans grant them additional strength and properties in battle. Killing one of these monsters allows the player to claim its Talisman for himself.
The standard and hardcore versions have the same core mechanics but slightly different items available.
The leagues started on December 11, 2015 along with the release of
Version 2.1.0
and ended March 4, 2016.
Characters that died in standard Talisman league respawned in the standard Talisman league, while those that died in hardcore Talisman league respawned in
Standard league
. At the end of the Talisman leagues, all characters and
stashes
were moved to Standard league and
Hardcore league
.
Modifier
All areas had:
Ancient Talismans possess monsters throughout Wraeclast
Items
Talismans
Main page:
Talisman
Talismans are a type of
amulet
. All talismans are
corrupted
when dropped, so they cannot be modified. They have special implicit modifiers more powerful than ordinary amulets and can be normal, magic, rare, or unique. For the full list of talismans possession effects and other related information, see
Talisman
.
Shared between HC and SC
These base items were exclusive to the standard and hardcore Talisman leagues:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageID"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
After the league end, magic and rare Talisman can be acquired from other game mechanics, read
Talisman
article for details.
Standard league-exclusive
These base items were exclusive to the standard Talisman league:
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
Lua error: Error: invalid field alias "items._pageName"; aliases cannot contain dots or quotes..
After the league end, magic and rare Talisman can be acquired from other game mechanics, read
Talisman
article for details.
